user_comment: Cute and comfy! These jeans are SUPER cute, stylish, stretchy and sooo comfortable! I’m in love with these jeans and how soft they are - they are just as comfortable as some of my yoga pants! The color is good and they are the perfect length on me (I’m 5’4”) however they look nothing like the ones on the model - she is definitely way taller. They are just the right amount of flare and look good with booties! Nice addition to my fall wardrobe!!
I love these jeans! I usually wear a size 12 and I’m 6 feet tall. I got a size 9/36. They are a little loose and plenty long. Definitely order a smaller size. But the fit is great and I love them.

Tall girl approved jeans! Fits comfortably, honestly could’ve ordered a size smaller since I prefer a pretty snug fit on my waist. The material seems durable enough, and the length seems to be true to 36” inseam. I’m 6’1 and have accepted my fate to wear jeans that cut off at the ankle, but if these pants last I will definitely be getting more. I ordered the dark indigo, but these don’t appear to be the color that I ordered, which is fine overall. Darker denim tends to fit differently than light color, so possibility of ordering a smaller size for the Brianna style jeans is likely.

Very Comfortable Jeans! These are a really comfortable pair of jeans. They are in the more current style, being on the shorter side and flared. I have to say I really like the fit. They have a good bit of stretch to them, so the size 18 fit me fine, and that's my usual size for most jeans. I like the color, which is a bit lighter, but it is still what I would consider a standard jean color. They are relatively high-waisted, but due to all the stretch, they are not going to "hold" you in the way jeans with stiff material will. I like the length on me, I'm 5'6 for reference, and they hit just at my ankle. My only pet peeve is that, basically, the entire pair of jeans is standard with no distressing, except for a random tiny spot on one of the back pockets. I think because this is the only spot for any distressing, it looks more like an actual tear or rip than intentional distressing. It's not a big deal, but that's the only thing I don't like about these jeans.

Stretchy, comfortable, and stylish These are an incredibly comfortable pair of jeans. They are denim with a lot of spandex, so they got snug to your body. I am mostly 10 but sometimes 8, and I ordered a size 10 bad on the size chart. These fit great. I like the black color as well. My only gripe is there is some "distressing" on the back pocket that I didn't know would be there. I'm older and I don't like the distressed look. Thankfully, these decorative holes are only in the back pocket and not all over the pants. I am 5'3" and these are just barely ankle pants. I don't mind because crops aren't my favorite. These pants can be dressed up or down and are a great staple to have in your closet.

Stylish, Comfortable & Affordable I purchased these pants to wear to concert and I absolutely loved them. They are really comfortable & they look great. Only thing I would suggest, is making the back pockets deeper (the back pockets are a little shallow and I could not fit my cell phone in the pocket) but other than that... the waist was comfortable with the elastic band, the pants length was perfect and they looked great... not to mention they were affordable. I plan to get another pair. I am 5'8" and weigh approximately 198 lbs and the XXL was a perfect fit for me.

Stretchy, thin material, great price point. Just got these, so this is based off of unwashed straight outta the bag. I bought an XL. I am 5'9" and between 170-180 lbs bottom heavy. Big tummy, hips, thighs and butt. They are comfy and stretchy. They fit just above my ankles. I would definitely order more if they had Jean or black color in my size. The material is fairly thin. I have a lot of cellulite dimples and they definitely show more in these white pair than a denim colored pair I received through a different retailer. The denim pair are also heavier material than these white ones I purchased on Amazon. I go commando and I feel like you can almost see through them. I certainly wouldn't wear any type of colored panties underneath. I think some nylons underneath would help smooth some of the dimples. Over all I think these are pretty awesome for a $13 pair of pants. I've played more for pants I've liked less. I think the top you pair them with is gonna be what makes the difference if you are curvy.

A cheap petite’s dream? These are $10 pants, but are still cute, while they last. I am 5’3” and typically a size 6. If you are petite, you will be thrilled. It appears a lot of women didn’t notice the inseam is only 27” long. So, if you are over 5’4”, these will be short on you, and you may like that or style them by rolling them up. I like the placement of the pockets on my flat butt. The front pockets are fake. The waistband is nice and stretchy and I can pull on over my pooch. I wore them for about 7 hours today and they’re not uncomfortable, however, look at photos so you can see how the rips wear. The strings coming off are nylon, not denim so they ball up. I will just trim them. I shared photos of the stringy balls, the slits, and my outfit. My daughter thought I looked pretty cute. I paired these with booties, but would look cute with flats too. I would order more, but they’re sold out now. I do wonder how see through they would be in white... these black are pretty thick though :) Will wear them until they look like $10 pants.

Give them a try! I am 5'2" 168 lbs & purchased the large. I am on the right in my photo and I did give them a tiny double cuff, for height reference. They fit perfect! I'm actually surprised at how much I love them. They are not jeans at all. They are stretchy and thin and I really like that because I don't have to worry about them fading. I would not put these in the dryer. They WILL shrink. The cuts are kinda cheesy at a glance but by the end of my first wear, they started to shred, in a good way.

Really small, and shrunk a lot when washed. I ordered a large and I am 5’6 and 135, typically wear a size 7. Although I could get these on, they were pretty tight...which was fine, until I washed them and they shrunk so much that they’re uncomfortably tight. They also shrunk in length by several inches. The material is not denim, but some kind of nylon, which makes the tears look kinda ridiculous - more like slits. Honestly, I probably still would have worn them a few times if they hadn’t shrunk so badly. The nice things about them are the elastic waistband (I’m pregnant) and the real back pockets. I wouldnt buy again.

Enthusiastic Approval. Triumphant! I am so incredibly happy with this product, I had to take pictures and do a review. I don't do this for every purchase (I couldn't possibly) but when I have an experience this positive, I have to acknowledge it so I give credit where credit is due and also provide other shoppers with information to help them with a decision. I bought these pants because I was searching everywhere for pants to match this bright purple blazer I had bought and it is just impossible. I saw these, and I knew it was a gamble, but they showed up in the mail the next day, and it was exactly the same color and hue as it was pictured on the internet. It is a perfect match! Not only that, but the fit is absolutely perfect. I think these are quite possibly my most comfortable pants in my wardrobe now. They are stretchy enough to not need a zipper or button (the ones you see are fake) but the pockets are REAL! All four pockets are nice and deep and functional. The fabric is firm enough so that it doesn't wind up looking like leggings (they look like fancy jeans.) They are formal enough to wear with professional attire. They are the perfect length for me (5'2") and they do not fit too tightly around the waist (a problem I find with way too many pants and skirts. They fit just like a glove. In fact, I was shocked at how perfectly they fit. They were almost out of my price range, but I am very very glad that I sprang for a few extra bucks and I may purchase a few more pairs in different colors now that I see how great a product this is. For your reference, I am 5'2", 122 lbs, 31" waist.

More legging than jegging I'm 5'2 and 160 lb (which i carry all in front, not in my hips). I bought a large. As you can see, they are a little long on me so they bunch up a bit at the ankles. They are also really more legging than jean - clingy, thin cloth (but not see through for me). My husband said he didnt like the shape of these pants and I was like "Uh, thats the shape of my legs!"But I do love the choices of colors. I love the way this pair coordinates with the tunic I'm wearing and they arent too warm for warmer weather. I do wear this outfit to work, so I may consider getting more to coordinate with other tunics, but I'm not sure.

So comfortable!! I bought these for work, where I am required to wear khaki pants/shorts. The fact that they have functional front and back pockets was a HUGE selling point for me. I am 5'3 and between 125-130lbs. My waist was 30" at my time of purchase so I could not decide between small or medium because according to the size chart, I am at the upper spectrum of small (25 - 32) and at the lower spectrum for medium (28 - 38) and I really do fluctuate between those sizes depending on the brand. I ultimately went with small and they fit nicely with absolutely no squeezing of my body. I am so comfortable at work and because of the texture of the fabric, they look like form fitting pants rather than leggings. As you can see in the photo, the pants are too long for me, so I will stick to the capri style. I've worn them once a week for the past month and although I have followed the directions for washing and drying, the fabric is starting to look like its going to pill so I am not sure how much longer they will look nice. If I order more to wear outside of work, maybe I'll try hanging them to dry and see if that helps.

Looks like a Gucci This AUROLRAN belt feels top notch - reminds me of a Gucci. It came with a nice pull string pouch to store it in, love those for keeping my belts looking nice. I am 5’6, 140 pounds and size 6 fits perfectly. I got these with a 20% off coupon and a flash sale, so the value for the price is fantastic.

Super Cute But SUPER Stretchy I’m 5’5,” 165lbs but carry excess weight in my tummy. My waist is currently 36/37” and per the brand’s size guide provided I went with the sz 18. I’ve never been a sz 18 - I’m typically sz 14 or 32/33 waist in Free People jeans, but I love these jeans and wanted them to work. Turns out they fit! But due to how incredibly stretchy these are I definitely could’ve sized down to a 16. I still love the way these look and they’re a great dupe for the FP barrel jeans I’ve purchased in the past (and a lot less expensive). They did shrink some in the washer/dryer, but after a few hours they tend to stretch out again. I received these through Amazon Vine, but I love them so much I’m purchasing on my own a pair in a smaller size. Plus, at the time of writing this review (8/23/24) they’re on sale. Will update my review when I receive the smaller size. I do recommend these jeans, but keep in mind the stretch factor. The quality is excellent and I especially love how lightweight these are. It’s hot and humid here in South Florida, but these are jeans I could sport year round.

The Angel's Forever Young 360 Bootcut Jeans are perfect for my everyday lifestyle. I purchased these jeans as I have gained weight and all my other jeans cut into my waist and had less give than these jeans. The waistband and denim are soft and thick with enough stretch for comfort without losing its shape. After all we all need a little support when you are as curvy as I am. These do not change your shape like butt lifting jeans do. I will be doing more squats for that...lol. I would buy these again in a different color. I bought a size 6. My measurements are waist 37.5", booty 44", hips 43.5" and inseam 28.5". I could hem them, but I like that my ankles stay warm when I squat.

I'm 5"4" at 140 lbs and they're on the large side for me. I was hoping for something more form fitting for me. They are about 2" to long and I think a 4 would be better for me, but 6 seems to be the smallest on their size chart. They are definitely stretchy, but since they're already to large the stretch just looks baggy on me.

Super cute comfy YES!! Size 3 Fits my 39” butt!! I usually wear a size 3 junior length and stomach about 27”waist size,30 belt.. but sometimes feel like my butt is just smashed in.. these DONT Smash they smooth right on over! Yay ! Cute pockets too.. not a”mom” Jean. One star away for the stitching could possibly come out after awhile seems like maybe…

PERFECT length for my height My info: I am 5'9", 215 lbs, long legs, short torso, large tummy, normally a size 20 OR 2X. I had originally ordered the 20 Plus Long but they were baggy on me under the tummy and lower butt areas and the waist & legs areas were really loose, so I return those and ordered the 18 Plus Long. They are still a little baggy under the areas I mentioned above, which I would have to wear a long tunic to cover these areas. The waist & legs are more fitted but the length is PERFECT!! These are a true LONG as they reach exactly even with the bottom of my ankles. The fabric, IMHO, can be worn in ALL seasons; it's thick but not heavy thick, similar to jeans. The only negatives I have are, I wish there wasn't a zipper & buttons AND I wish they had belt loops. So in regards to belt loops, I will take these pants to a fabric store to match or closely match these fabrics so that I can sew belt loops on these pants.Edited to say: As you can see, these do not lift the butt!

Finally Long Enough!! Says the 6 ft woman. Having packed on the Covid 15, I was scared to get my actual size. I went with a 22, but now wish I had gone for the 20. At 6 foot, 265 pounds, it’s nearly impossible to find jeans long enough. I’ve been looking for the perfect pair of jegging‘s for over a year. These pants are thicker than leggings, but terrific. They don’t lift my bum, but at 55 I don’t care. They are super stretchy, Maybe if I had gone a size smaller, they would hold my stomach in more, but they don’t feel super strong. They are very comfortable. AND LONG ENOUGH! I will be keeping these for my fatty days, and will order another one in size 20, hoping for more tummy support. I love that they are truly high waisted (over my belly button), have four real sized pockets, and come below my ankles. I highly recommend. Don’t judge me on my pics, LOL.

Highly recommend. Stop flip flopping and just buy these! I LOVE these pants! Enough to deserve a thoughtful review. That said, let's start with the fit! The pants are advertised as butt lifting. I cannot personally confirm this, as i do not have a problem in that area, but i CAN confirm that the pants almost contour to your body. There's no bunching or baggy spots. The stretch is enough to provide a perfect fit but not too much that they become loose. I love the rise - I'm a sucker for high waisted pants 😍 These come right up about up to your belly button (which is my favorite height for high waisted pants). Super cute rise! Just make sure to measure yourself properly with this type of rise - there isn't gonna be a lot of wiggle room. I know my measurements well but i also try to keep in mind that a lot of women dont so i just want to put that reminder. Measure your waist at your belly button. Measure your hips at your thickest part. As for the length: I'm a little short - not quite 5'3 - so the pants are a tad long on me. However, if you're average height or like me and always wear heels, this wont be a problem. Plus, they're easily rolled at the bottom if needed. Over all these are very cute, comfortable and everything i waned. Black skinny jeans are a staple for me so I think I will be ordering another pair. I got a lot of complements on these (usually you don't get compliments on plain black pants lol).

Insanely inconsistent sizing. 3 stars for the pair that actually fit. I first reached out to the company on the 6th of January, to see if this could be rectified before I left a review using the email they had on the happy/unhappy card with my purchase. No reply. I bought 3 pairs, all the same style in a size 1. So, the very first pair I received are Amazing! The fit was excellent, the length, love love loved it. Those were the medium blues. Was so stoked to get the other two being delivered the following day. Thought I had finally found “the jeans” you just love to wear. Wow, just completely shocked at the inconsistency in sizing. The dark wash was fitting like a 0, a bit tight on my hips but I could still put them on. The light blue acid ones. Omg. I couldn’t even get them past my hips. It was like trying to put 00’s on and I was a bad can of busted biscuits. I’m 5’9, and 114lbs. You can see each measurement just getting smaller and smaller. Each stating they are a size 1. No, honey they are not. I now believe the other reviews. It’s just incredibly frustrating and annoying that even though they are the exact same style, just different colors, none of them are the same. I’ll update my review once they finally contact me.

I’m glad I didn’t size up. UPDATED: I added pictures I purchased the V3/ (high waisted with the 3 buttons) size 9/ in Dark Wash thinking they might end up being a little too small, but they’re actually a little loose (and very stretchy). So maybe the manufacturer updated sizing to be more true to size? (I see in the comments that a lot of people have had to size up one or two sizes.) Summary: Sizing? True to size for me. I’m 5’7” around 140lbs waist 29” hips 39-1/2” inseem about 32” and this size 9 will work even though it is a little loose fitting. High-Waisted? Yes. The pants sit above my belly button Butt-Lifting? Yes and no... They’re not fitted enough for me to notice any actual structural butt lifting, but I do believe the cut/ style around the butt is flattering (Sorry the picture isn’t better. Difficult to take a pic of your own butt!)

Favorite pant brand!! I read a lot of reviews saying how bad these are and sizing is off. While I agree, I followed the sizing guide and ordered a size smaller than I usually wear, and they fit perfect. I'm in love with these. So, do not hesitate to buy them. Worth the purchase This is the 3rd color I've purchased from Hybrid and I'm very pleased with all of them (olive, wine & now khaki). They are high waisted, long in length (which is great since I'm 5'10" and it's hard to find long lengths) and they are stretchy. I'm usually a 10, maybe 11 is size depending on the maker bit I got these in a size 9 and they fit amazing so I would say they run a little big. I feel like they hold your tummy in and lift your bum to look amazing at the same time. The only reason I gave it 4 stars is because the khaki color in the listing is not what I received. The color in their khaki description is more of a taupe and the color I received is closer to their "tobacco" color. However, they fit great and I'm planning to keep them.

So I am very pleased with this product and consider the quality and value for $ to be great.

Long jeans that don't work for most body types. I'll start with I'm 5'4 170 with curves. I first purchased size 32 as recommended by Amazon and the sizing chart. They were large enough I could pull them off without unbuttoning them. Sized down to the 31 which is the size in the pics. The fabric and the top part of the jeans is the only reason these jeans get 2 stars. The waist sits nice and when I sit it doesn't do the stupid gap in the back. I think the cut and sizing all works well until you get to the calves and the inseam. These are long. So much that I had on 4in heels and they still were on the floor if I dared wear them with the heels. I was nervous my heels would get caught in all the excess fabric of the calf area. I get straight leg but at 9in wide (when the thighs are 10 there's some taper) I felt like I was in the oh so popular JNCO jeans. So I tried with tennis shoes. Again my shoe could get lost in the leg causing a trip hazard. So I arolled them up twice which only caused more bulk. If you're not statuesque or planning on cutting these into shorts I wouldn't recommend. Comfortable, a few issues I ordered these jeans in size medium in the black, grey color. I'm 5'8" 125lbs with a fairly straight figure, and they fit very comfortably. The material has a nice stretch to it and fits snugly but not too tight like some high-rise jeans do. I measured the rise at 10inches. The inseam was 29inches which is a little short for my height, so I'll probably wear them mostly with boots in the future. The cut is wide leg but has kind of a slight flare and the leg opening is 10inches. A few small issues are that the jean material doesn't go down into the pocket at all so when I stand up after sitting, I have to re-tuck the pockets. these are regular sized pockets, so that's good. Also, two of the three buttonholes are a little small and I have to kind of force the buttons in. Still, overall, these are good jeans and a cute and comfortable style.

I gave them three stars for being long Good points: Very deep black (and they come with a warning that the colour might rub off on lighter textiles!) VERY stretchy if that's what you want Nice and long in the legs. I'm 5'11" and could wear boots or heels with these Modern boot cut (more on that later) Drawbacks: The FIT: I always wear a 12 in Lee jeans and these were a bit tight. I could wear them if I didn't mind the muffin top due to the fact they are smaller than any of my other jeans and the "midrise" is very low (to me, at least). If you are looking for mid-to-high, really, like me, do not buy these. The STRETCH: VERY stretchy. The kind of stretch that snaps in to hug your body no matter what. And, I don't know why, but that sort of pants always seems to get folds in the backs of the thighs (the front, too, if they are loose, but these are not). They are also very stifling in the heat. If, like me, you live somewhere where it is 30 degrees Celsius much of the year, do not order these. You're welcome! (Looked it up, that's 86 in Fahrenheit.) The CUT: As I said, these are modern boot cut. Maybe I am really looking for flares. 20 years ago, what we called boot cut flared out right below the knees and looked great. These grab me right down to mid-calf (so everyone can clearly see how knock-kneed I am - if my legs were straight, I'd be even taller!). Then they flare waaaaaay out. I find that pretty ugly on me. I guess they could work with boots if you have small calves (I do not). They are indeed huge through the ankles, but I think they would be pretty snug over boots in the lower calf. Also, they are on the low side of midrise, which is another huge negative for high-waisted me. Hope this helps!

Stylish & Comfy Wide-Leg Jeans. I got these jeans for my daughter & they look adorable on her. She especially loves how stretchy & super comfy they are. Sizing might run a bit small. She’s a size 6, which the description equates to a "small," but I decided to order up 1 size as a precaution & opted for the "medium." Thankfully, that was the right decision. They fit her perfectly. The high waist is flattering. They’re fitted on the hips & backside without being too snug. Although they’re wide-leg, they don’t look baggy, maintaining a stylish shape. These jeans have a sturdy zipper, metal button, & back pockets, giving them that classic denim jeans look. However, the material is thinner than your average denim & even thinner than other stretchy denim I’m used to. Despite the thinner fabric, you can’t see through them in the white color, so it’s not a major concern. My daughter is 5'7", & the bottom hem lands just beneath her heel, which makes them look long with flat sandals. However, they pair well with shoes that have a raised sole or heel, like boots & wedges. She did mention they’re a bit long below the zipper, causing a slight bunching when she sits. Fortunately, this improved after washing, as I expected since stretchy denim tends to draw up a bit. Although the care instructions advise against tumble drying, I did toss them in the dryer on a low, wrinkle-free setting & they held up just fine. Overall, these jeans are stylish & fit great. I’d recommend getting precise measurements & comparing them to the size chart, as you may need to size up. The denim is decent quality, though I’m a bit concerned about how long they’ll last given the thinner material. But mostly, she’s thrilled with how comfortable they are.

Heavy denim stretch waist look at model closely 5’7” 195- 200lbs Size 18 is too big because I thought the regular size 16 would be too small. Should have bought regular size. I liked: * Heavy weight denim 70% cotton. * Stretch 28.5% polyester and 1.5 % elastic-elastine * Loose fit in belly and legs * Not tight on ankles * Real pocket hold a cellphone iPhone 13 Pro * Not too hot - not too cold * Very comfortable on the skin after washing. I did not like the length. I know they are supposed to be at the ankle. They can be rolled up to look stylish. This is great for business casual days at work and for nurses who wear regular clothes but still need to do patient care. Nice to not burst out of pants while bending. I purchased mine too large. I was worried that the belly would be too tight. The elastine lets the waist stretch without being granny style elastic. I recommend offering your actual size. I would buy again. I will keep these because they are not jeggings and can still be worn baggy style. I recommend a figure defining top to give you a shape because the pants are baggy. I will keep these size 18 and order another pair sized to my regular size 16 and then style like the model. Keep slumping down! A bit mad I so hastily took off the tag on these, but my main issue is that they keep sliding down! I’m 25 weeks, 5’6, 190 lbs pre-pregnancy and usually a size 10-12 with 41-42 inch hip, so I ordered a large. Having worn under-the-bump jeans and finding that they were hitting in an uncomfortable spot, I wanted a pair of pants that go over the bump with front pockets so I can hook my work badge. Maternity pants with front pockets appear to be a rarity. These pants keep slipping down! The legs feel fine, although they run long so I need to roll/cuff them, but the waist is not staying in place. I feel like the bottom of the panel is constantly falling down to my crotch instead of staying above it. There’s no belt loops either, so it’s awkward to try to “hike up” the pants constantly. I’m not sure if sizing down would work, given that the legs are perfectly fine for me in the large, but these pants were not the 10/10 I was hoping for. Maybe they’re best for mid to late third trimester. Included a pic when they are first hiked up and another once I’ve been walking around to show the slump!

Not relaxed/loose enough to be called “boyfriend jeans” but decent for the price. I am 5’5” and 127 lbs. I normally wear a size 4 or 27 in jeans, medium bikini bottoms, and small in shirts. I was really torn which size to go with and decided on the medium recommended by the size chart. Let’s just go ahead and get the negative out of the way. When I think of “boyfriend jeans” I expect straight and loose legs but fits at the waist. The legs of these jeans are not baggy enough to be considered true boyfriend jeans. This pair in particular is quite a bit more fitted than the model photo. I would classify this style as distressed relaxed leg. Now let’s move on to judging the material. These are soooo stretchy. I wore them for a full day of running around town and they never lost shape or loosened up. They look tight in my photos but in real life the waist line does have some gapping at the spine and is barely touching my abdomen. Even though the size chart recommended a small, I am extremely glad I went with the medium. I think these jeans are stretchy enough that the small would have fit but they would have been super tight skinny jeans. The material of the light blue is slightly scratchy before a run through the washer. I don’t trust them to survive many rounds through the dryer but they did survive a tumble on the low heat/delicate cycle because I was trying to soften them up a bit. I will probably hand dry in the future to keep the fraying in tact. The paint splatters on the jeans are white and very much paint (not bleach) so they could flake off over time. Recommendation: Cheap enough to be worth a shot. The material is thin, stretchy, and ripped so these will make great summer jeans. Don’t expect them to be really loose unless you are willing to size up but keep in mind that the waist may be too big for you if you do size up. This pair of jeans just became my go-to for weekend road trips because they are stretchy enough for the car but also cute. I was comfortable and cool enough wearing them for a full day in the Texas heat.

soooo cute for the short girls! but they do have some faults… Please excuse my dirty mirror, but these pants are soooo cute and perfect in person! I really love them and they are embroidered and not printed on so they look high quality although the quality could be better for 50 dollars. Now while I like how they look, I didn’t give 5 stars because they do SHRINK quite a bit like some of the reviews suggest. I suggest sizing up one or two sizes for this reason. I am 5ft tall exactly and I am usually a size 14-16 in jeans. I usually would have gone with the XL but due to the reviews about shrinkage I went with the XXL. When I tried it on they were long and little loose on the waist so I threw them in the wash. They shrunk to the most perfect size! Unfortunately these pants are not for the tall girlies, again I am very short and you can see after shrinking they hit right above my ankles which is perfect for me, but not for taller girls. I am worried that washing them again may shrink them even more which would suck but hopefully this is all they will shrink. Definitely not pants you will want to wash often haha.

5’4” Thick Booty Mama - These Jeans Are It! I have been abusing the crap out of these jeans for about a year now. I’m back to buy more and thought I’d leave a review. These jeans are so comfortable - they fit in all the right places, yet are flexible enough to move around and do all my tasks and responsibilities comfortably, even meditating with my little one! Best big-booty-big-thigh jeans eva! Fantastic fit. I’m 6’ tall 201 lbs w/30” inseam 34” waist 44” hips 28” thighs!!👀👀👀 I am ALWAYS in two sizes up to fit my thighs and butt. This leaves me so much gap and nothing to hold up the pants on my giant thighs. Add large calves, and you basically just walk around holding your pants up or wear a belt and try to tuck and conceal all the extra waistband. These jeans are for curvy girls with a waist—OR NOT. My friend tried them on. She carries her weight through the middle and is maybe one size up from me, was able to put them on and zip them up. I was wearing a 16 and ordered a 16. They went on like butter and fit every curve of me. Very comfortable and easy to bend, sit and move. They loosen up quick, so grab a belt after first day wear. They’ll need a hot-water tightening often. I went ahead and ordered a 14 in the light shade. Both fit so well, I’m debating the12 now. 🤔 Stylish? So many compliments already. As for stamina—how well will they hold up over time? 🤷‍♀️ I’ll try to update in a few months.

Comfortable and cute! I have not worn jeans for 5 years, but these are comfortable and cute. The reasons why are-1) I have Interstitial Cystitis which means I cannot endure any pressure on my pelvic floor, and 2) I gained a lot of weight during CoVid lockdown so nothing fit. I’m finally down a little. Currently I am 5’7”, 178pounds, and hips measure 45”. I think that makes me size 14-16 depending on fit. I got the XL. They are not at all tight, even a bit loose around my hips, but I have not washed them-just tried them on. These are the Classic Blue and they have good color, some contrasting lightening in strategic areas, and zero smell-which I only mention because other reviews had concerns about chemical scent. You could probably get away with a heeled sandal if wearing in late Spring, Summer, or early Fall. They will look like capris on tall people. I’m a regular 30” inseam and wouldn’t be able to wear any closed-toe shoes except flat tennis shoes (think converse low tops). Love that they’re machine washable, and I don’t think they’ll shrink too much. They seem to be pre washed. Hope that’s to you all!

Comfortable, true to size wide leg jeans Shopping for jeans is usually something that I prefer to do in person but lately it’s easier to risk buying online due to limited selection in most stores. This was a first and a great surprise. These jeans are really well made, they are true to size and are forgiving enough to weight fluctuations without excessive Spandex. They have a wide leg cut, mostly considered high waisted and have a “paper bag” waist with button closure and a bit of elastic on the back. There is a string to cinch the waist and emphasize the “paper bag design” but it is more of an accessory than a true functional part. Fabric has a nice weight and there are 6 color options, mine was Azure Mood which is like a light denim blue; overall they flow nicely without being too exaggerated. For reference, I’m 5’6” tall, curvy Latina who carry most of her weight on her lower half but still have a defined waist; size L fit me. There is a chance that I’ll remove the cord at the waist for a cleaner look or just change it for ribbon in a closer or even contrasting color. This style of jeans is a throw back to Y2K era, and can be styled with cropped tops, bodysuits, tops tucked half in/half out and may be your go to pair of jeans during Spring, Summer and milder days of Fall. If I have to be over critical of this product, I’ll agree with other customers who noted that tall people may have to proceed with caution, because they run a tad short. Overall 4.5 stars. Pros: • True to size, wide leg jeans • Fabric is soft, not too stretchy. Jeans come in 6 different shades. Cons: • Jeans may run a tad short, buyer beware.

I like I love a wide leg pant and these are cute. I am 5'5" and weigh approximately 150. I think I'm a pretty true size 12 and I ordered the large. The fit is true for me. My waist has not fully recovered from my last pregnancy and is thicker than what I'm used to, so there's a bit more snugness in the waist than I would prefer, but nothing unbearable. I consider these pants comfortable with the elastic give. I am not super leggy and this length is about perfect for me. I usually struggle with pants running under my heels in a regular length, and this length is absolutely perfect for me. So, if you are tall and leggy, plan on these being a cropped pant. Perhaps they were even meant to be a cropped pant, but they work as a full length for me. I would definitely order again.

Comfortable and stretchy I love the stretch and comfort of these jeans. They’re form fitting but the material is soft and extremely flexible. They’re the jeggings with the 70s flare/bell bottom look. The material is thin however, so even though they have a lot of stretch, I can imagine they can easily wear down after so many machine washes. It sits high on the waist which can be firm flattering for curvier figures. I like that it has pockets that have a good amount of depth on both the sides and back. I ordered a size XS as I’m 5 ft 108lbs. It fits more like a small and the hem runs past my feet so I’ll likely have to tailor them or wear higher heels. $41 dollars is a bit steep for the thinness of the material but wearing comfortable jeans with this amount of stretch could be a flex.

A must have in the every color! I own these cargo pants in just about every color, and I was shocked by the quality of the material. The fabric is smooth and gives the pants a tailored look, with an elastic band in the back that prevents gapping. There isn’t much stretch, but they are true to size. I typically wear a size 16 or 17 depending on the brand, and I purchased these in a size 18—they fit perfectly. The pants have functioning pockets and a real zipper, unlike some that just have decorative details. In my opinion, they are tall-friendly. At 5’6”, they look great with heels. These can easily be dressed up for an elevated casual look or paired with sneakers or flats and a tank top for a neat, casual style.

Really cute and comfortable I was surprised how much I liked these jeans. I am 5'8" 125lbs and I ordered size 6. I went off the size chart and got the six because of the waist measurement. These fit well, but I probably could have gotten the size 4 for a more fitted look and still had a comfortable fit. The material is soft and stretchy, and also not too thin. These are straight wide leg and high waist. They also have a line detail down the front which is elongating and flattering. The length was ok, it hit right at my ankles, but I was kind of hoping they would be longer and slouchier like in the web pic. So, if you are above 5'8 they might hit short unless you are going for the ankle or cropped look. They have actually useable pockets on the front and back, as well. Overall, a really comfortable and cute pair of jeans, but pay attention to the length and you might want to size down.

Such a surprise! I’ve seen the trend of barrel jeans/pants and thought to my old millennial self that that trend would never be something I’d put in my wardrobe. SO WORTH IT!! I’ve always been obsessed with leopard print everything, so when I found out FP had leopard print barrel jeans I was stoked until I realized they were out of stock so I opted for these instead. I’m normally a M - L (145lbs 5’4”) and didn’t want to risk them being too small so I got the L and I can’t wear them without a belt. So if you’re between sizes, go with the size down. The button piece above the zipper does seem to be loosening but that’s fixable.

I bought these jeans for my kids. They like trendy clothes, they wear jeans with holes in them, and I thought these are a shoe in. The jeans arrived yesterday, and surprisingly my girls weren’t too interested. I think because it’s summer and they’re thinking shorts and mini skirts rather than jeans. Big win for me! I cannot believe how amazing I look in these jeans. Now, I’m not the type of person to compliment myself pretty much ever. I’ll be the first person to point out my wrinkles, jowls, saggy bat wing arms, and lumpy cellulite allll over. But these jeans make me look like a movie star. I don’t know what kind of magic was sewn into the fabric, but they’re comfy with just the right amount of stretch, they stay on all day… So they’re stretchy, but they snap back where they need to, they aren’t low rise, but they don’t go up to my chest. I’m telling you, these are perfect. I think the only people who might not love these jeans as much as I do are taller people. I say that only because I have a 30 inch inseam… a lot of my friends have a 32 or 33 inch inseam and these jeans would be a little bit too short for their liking. Some women prefer the ankle length look, and this would definitely work for that. These jeans are absolutely perfect on me and I’m going to be looking at some of the other colors as soon as I’m done writing this review.

5ft 4, Petite Friendly! I Moonwalked down the hallway! Oh my word it’s a size 4~~!! It’s really difficult to find a size 4 in stores and in general jeans that I’m not walking on because I’m a petite. The hems are right at the top of my feet. I’m 5’4”, Waist 26, Hip 36, and 107lbs. The elephant in the room needs to be addressed—yes the “light blue” is a little darker than in seller’s pictures. These are super soft and stretchy with a feeling that a distant relative of it could have been a Jegging! Lol I usually wear size 5. Size 5 is too big around the waist but it’s the looseness that I like in the legs. Runs big These jeans are so cute! Initially when I ordered, I went with amazons suggestion (which is usually pretty spot on), and ordered a medium. They were huge, but I loved them so I reordered in a small. Honestly, after getting them and wearing them, I could have done an extra small. They are a mid-rise fit, super stretchy, and comfortable (like no joke ladies if you’re still dropping it like it’s hot, these are for you). They are the perfect cropped length (I am 5’7), the white is not see through (I’m wearing magenta underwear in the pics), and I am seriously obsessed with the flared bottom detail. I was really hoping to just pop these in the dryer for some shrink action, but the label specifically says no dryer so I’m rolling with the looser fit. These definitely look more high end than they are, and I’m probably going to order the Jean version because I like them so much. I definitely recommend, but would suggest sizing down. For reference I am pretty universally a size 3 or small in most pants.

Tall girls wanted Here I go again, ordering pants I know will not suit a vertically-challenged chick as myself (5’1”). Sometimes it works out considering how you wear it, yes? These jeans, size small in medium blue, were not the case. I had to wear them below my waist/belly button. Otherwise, they would be too loose, requiring a belt.There is some stretch to these pants so I could pull them on and off without unbuttoning. Not that it matters anyway since there is extra fabric everywhere. I think if the hemline didn’t flare, these jeans would have been ok on me. They remind me of another pair I ordered here on the Amazon. But those worked better being wide-legged.I’ll keep these for now and hope I grow five inches overnight. The denim is medium weight. The quality is comparable to Gap. In the meantime, I’ll pair them with anything but flats or flip flops. Fake it til you make it as the fashion world says it.

Fit well and comfortable The picture shows one of many ways I plan on wearing these comfy pants. They have just the right amount of stretch. I washed mine on cold/delicates and hung them dry. If you have an apple shape, you will need to size up. I’m 5’4”, 160 pounds and the medium fit well. If I weighed a smidge over 160, they wouldn’t work. The fabric is soft and wasn’t see through with my skin tone underwear. The pockets sit high on the front and while functional, are a little awkwardly placed for truly holding items. There are pockets on the back that work great for normal use. This will be a staple in my wardrobe. I really enjoyed wearing them.

However I also ordered the black in medium but they’re so big they ripple on my legs, so I need a small. Super inconsistent, the trial and error is worth it though!

Mine came w/HOLES + they fit LOOSE on me They are very stretchy with lots of room for progressing pregnancy, I guess. I am 5'3" / 120 lbs pre-pregnancy and usually wear size small. They hit at the ankle on me and are a too little loose to be a true "skinny" jean. I like my skinny jeans to look more like leggings. You can see how loose they look in my webcam pics. For temporary pants to get you through the 6 months you need something to wear, they seem okaaay. On the pair I received, I noticed at least 2 holes (1 large, 1 small) in the back, on the stretchy part near the hip & above where it meets the jeans. This was straight out of the package; I have not washed them yet. So I guess I lost the quality lottery with my pair. I was desperate for pants though so I am planning on repairing them myself with a needle/thread. If the rips were in the front, I might be less lazy about returning them. It's been difficult for me to find maternity pants that are small enough, but somehow these aren't as loose as others. It's still pretty early in my pregnancy (not quite halfway yet) so I don't expect the belly to fit just yet, but they are fairly loose around my thighs/hips so I feel I need to constantly pull them up. Not the most comfortable, but better than no pants. I'm hoping I like them better later in my pregnancy when my belly can help to hold them up. If after washing they are still quite loose, I'll just take them in a bit along the leg seams so I don't feel so frumpy.

A Well Made Reversible Leather Belt... For Me I'm currently replacing old belts in my wardrobe accessories, and I really like reversible ones because I like the two color options in one belt. So, getting this AWAYTR Reversible Leather Belt was an easy decision for me. The first thing for me to say is that before I ordered it, I noticed that its design looked more like something I would wear instead of something my wife would, even though it's advertised first as a woman's belt. Once I was able to look at it in person, the style's definitely something I could wear. With that observation out of the way, here is my assessment of the belt. It is well made with good thick leather, a stylish belt buckle, and the stitching is really good. Reversing the belt is easy to do, and it locks solidly in place. The length of the belt can be cut down for more of a proper fit, if necessary. I found this info on the product page (Instructions for doing this did not come with the belt). I cut about 2 1/2" from the length to give me the correct fit for my 34" waist. Also, the two colors, black/coffee, are exactly the same as belts that I'm getting rid of, so I am really satisfied with this new belt.

Also my inseam is about 30" and the length is perfect, if you have longer legs these would be too short. I hope this helps.

Pleasantly Surprised I had zero expectations for these, but I had them recommended by an Amazon influencer and decided to give them a try! First of all, I sized UP. I wanted a super baggy look, so after reading reviews I went with a Large instead of my usual Small. I’m glad I did! They’re super high rise, baggy in the legs but tighter in the waist. However, I can still pull them down lower and wear them slouchy. There is no stretch in the fabric, FYI. Length is long on me at 5’2” but I kinda like it. Print and fabric quality is actually really nice. But please note: they smell AWFUL right out of the bag. Like, truly so bad. Still keeping them! :)

Great quality, inaccurate sizing. This is a stylish belt that is very well made. The buckle is secure and the material feels lovely. The loop itself makes this an item that is easy to dress up with empire waist dresses and outfits. I love this belt with the exception of the sizing being a little off. I ordered this in a large based on the provided size chart of my waist measuring at 33”. The belt buckles on the first hole provided but I could go in another notch if it was available to me. This leaves almost 10” of extra belt hanging at the end with no additional belt loop to keep it from sagging. The large belt measures 44” overall from buckle to end of belt tail. I am 5’2”, 140lbs with a 33” waist and size 8 pants, size small dresses. I could have easily done the medium and maybe even the small given the extra 10” of belt left. Overall. It is an absolutely lovely belt, high quality, comfortable and very stylish but if you are on the fence about sizing, size down.

Not As Described! I had ordered the navy pair of these leggings in M/L a week ago & absolutely loved them! They fit perfectly & are so warm & cozy. So I decided to order another pair in the skin color & they are COMPLETELY different! Different style, thickness & sizing. The skin has seams down the inside of the legs. Navy are seamless. Skin has 5" waistband, navy is 3". Skin is 8% spandex, navy is 15%. Skin has no front or back seam going up the front & back. Skin has regular leg openings at ankles, navy has sort of a stirrup. The skin pair did still fit me (5'3", 130lbs), but with less stretch than the navy pair. The fleece inside the skin pair is much thinner & not plush like the navy ones. However, the shade of this skin pair is a much better shade of "skin" than the shade shown on the website. Because I liked the shade so much I contemplated keeping them (they actually match my skin), but they were too thin to wear as pants & although they fit more like tights, the seams running down the inside of the legs made them unacceptable to me to wear as tights with my sweater dress. These had to go back & I reordered the skin colored ones from a different vendor. I'm hoping the other vendor actually sends the product advertised & doesn't pull a bait & switch as this vendor did.

I will try ordering the small size, but the m/l size is already way too short, so I have very low confidence they will fit next time. Do yourself a favor and keep searching

Comfortable and stylish! Here's my wife for this review:I wanted a pair of jeans that I could be comfortable in and look a little up to date with the trends and I didn't want them to be blue necessarily. I saw these offered in a few colors and loved the olive green color. I also loved that these are high waisted and have a loose fitting to these. The straight legged barrel look is popular right now and honestly it's a comfortable style to wear. I ordered them in a size 10 which is my go to size. I'm 5'6 and 160 pounds. I think they could be a little baggier but they fit me in the waist nicely. They're a LOT of stretch to these jeans which adds to the comfort. I can roll the pant legs up to create a wide hem which is in style today or I can leave them long which fits me like a good hemmed pair of jeans would be. I do have shorter legs than most people so perhaps this would be above the ankle on most people which is also the style today. I also love that you could just cut the hem and leave it raw which is also the style of today!I haven't worn them for a long time so I don't know how the stretch factor is. Great if you're less endowed For reference I'm 5'4", 126 lbs, 29" waist, 37" hips with a smaller backside and I ordered these in size 6 according to their size chart, the solid green color. Just meh So I was in a pinch to order shorts for my upcoming road trip honeymoon, and I made the mistake of ordering 3 of these shorts in different colors.I'm 5'6", 122 lbs, waist 26", hips 34".. small fits comfortably. The shorts are definitely stretchy and in my honest opinion, overly-frayed.. look good from the back and the side. However, the front is just sub-par. It's almost as it the shorts were cut out from men's jeans.. too much extra fabric by the crotch. Therefore, when you take a step, they bunch up in a very unflattering way. It makes it look like I have external bits, if you know what I'm saying.!Seeing as they're still comfortable and don't look toooo hideous and I simply just don't have enough time to experiment with other jeans right now, I'll keep them. But I definitely won't purchase these again.. and in fact, it's scarring me from making future Amazon clothing purchases again.

MY NEW FAVORITE PANTS!!!! Ok so I’ve been wanting some overalls for a while now and I finally took the leap! They were super tight in the midsection and felt weird when I was sitting, so I was nervous to give overalls another try. But THESE ONES!? Oh my god. I usually change out of my jeans and into sweatpants the second I get home, but I wore these all night until I went to bed because they are just so freaking cozy. On top of that, they have tons and TONS of POCKETS!!! DEEP POCKETS LADIES, YOU HEARD THAT RIGHT!!!!!!! I’m talking about FIT YOUR ENTIRE PHONE in ‘em pockets!!! I could pass away. I usually wear a 6 in jeans so I got a medium and I will say they are a little loose, but I like that personally for me. If you want them more form fitting, I’d size down. But if you’re like me and you want a grunge-y 90’s look to layer sweaters under, these are for you! I have two cats who shed their light hair over EVERYTHING and I was a bit worried about getting black, but these were really to lint-roll the next day I wore them too. I’m also 5’8” and they fit really well in the legs so I imagine if you are any shorter you might find yourself cuffing the legs. Anyway, I have no complaints. Favorite comfy pants. I will be wearing these ALL WINTER!!!

FANTASTIC denium Capri pants I LOVE LOVE these Capris and I am So happy I ordered them!! I ordered the xl. For reference my height is 5'10" & 190lbs. I like the jean style, the pockets and the zipper (tried other faux leather pants with only button fermeture and didn't like it) I read in other comments that the material is noisy and I have to agree with that, BUT nothing that a little mineral oil rub cannot fix. Besides, I am sure it won't be so obvious at a party. I also appreciate the material looks, not latex shine looking. I chose the straight legs cut for it's versatility; it can be worn with high heels as well as tucked in boots without the bulk. I didn't notice any smell. A little pricey in my opinion for faux leather (even with a discount) but I had been wanting those pants for a long while so... 🤷🏽‍♀️ I finally gave in, and I don't regret it! 😁 Also, sorry the photos are not the best quality, I need a better phone!

Well made but... Hey Amazon. This is an entirely unbiased and unpaid for product review. I got these for a cosplay I was doing (Pearl from Steven Universe). I originally meant to cut these into shorts however I kept them long because of weather. These came within a day with my student Prime which was excellent. The product was very well packaged and immediately I noticed how soft these were. They were the texture of a very thin velvet almost and the color stayed saturated even at my joints. Trying them on they fit all the way down to NY ankles. I'm 5'2" so I didnt really have any concerns, however, I believe a taller person would have problems with leg length because even for a short person these didn't go awfully long. Now although the material was very very soft it was also very thin. My butt was covered in my costume however I wouldn't recommend wearing these (at least the light colors) by themselves. Wearing standard underwear the pattern and crease of the underwear was very obvious and looked pretty bad. I think even with a thong it'd be way too obvious. So if you plan to wear these by themselves get a darker pair because the lighter pair won't satisfy your needs. With them being so thin they didn't keep in heat very well either and I was pretty cold even inside. Also, I developed a small hole near my crotch after dancing in them for a while, probably due to constant stretching. Overall I believe these are decent quality. I would consider buying a black pair as leggings if the material would be made a little thicker. If you need something for under a skirt or shorts then these are absolutely perfect. TDLR; Soft and well made but too thin

Fits nice, stretchy, dark dark black I ordered three different types of Levi’s trucker jackets all in size Large (recommended size) for a side by side comparison. Please note that the 3 trucker jackets DO NOT COME WITH A HOODIE. I was simply making sure I could layer a hoodie underneath the denim jackets as this is how I like to wear them. All 3 Large sized jackets passed my hoodie test and Large felt like an accurate size recommendation to the style of each jacket. The sleeve length was long and perfect for me. If you’re looking for a more “form fitting” jacket, order the original trucker in your normal size (it does have stretch if you’re between sizes), order the Ex-Boyfriend a size smaller (no stretch.. watch out for arm length though), and skip the Baggy trucker altogether or try ordering it two sizes smaller (no stretch.. watch out for arm length changes ordering small sizes). My measurements: Height 5’8” - Weight 155lbs - Shoulder to Shoulder 17" - Bust 38" - Waist 28” - Hips 40". All of the Levi’s denim trucker jackets have inside “pockets” which is a feature I have always loved from Levi’s denim jackets. If you notice from the photos, each jacket is about 2” longer than the next. The Original trucker jacket being the shortest length and darkest wash; pictured in the first photo, all the way to the left (top & bottom) of the second photo and all the way to the right in the third photo. It is meant to be roomy but more form fitting, while the other two styles are meant to be baggy. I was still able to layer the Original trucker over my hoodie comfortably in my recommended size. While the Original trucker fit nicely and probably would look best over dresses out of the 3, I chose the Ex-Boyfriend because I already own a form fitting denim jacket and wanted something different. I liked the Original trucker the least because it was so dark, had all black buttons, and looked too new and clean. This sounds weird, but all my black jeans are all faded, so I preferred the faded washes on the other two black trucker jackets. The Baggy Trucker and Ex-Boyfriend trucker also have no stretch and have contrasting brownish brass buttons which felt more vintage. Notice the Ex-Boyfriend jacket has buttons on the right “men’s side” while both the Original Trucker and Baggy Trucker have buttons on the left “women’s side”. I hope my review helps you decide!

A must have wardrobe staple! Love this jacket! Hits right at my hips and has good feel in weight but not too heavy; just perfect for transition from summer to autumn. The color is in between dark denim and light denim. It has a slight stretch that I noticed most in the lower area of the last button. I am a classic Apple shape, stand 4’11.5”, weigh 155 lbs. and purchased a Medium. I was able to button it all the way including area around my tummy which is where I carry most of my weight. It fit like a glove with just enough give that I didn’t feel I couldn’t breathe in it or that it was too tight. Inside it also looks like there are two rather large hidden pockets on left & right side. You could easily fit an iPhone inside - see the pics I included. I have bigger arms and found that the upper sleeves were very roomy & would fit great for those gals with larger arms. I love the slanted side pockets on the front. Don’t wait on this - purchase it already! You will not regret it and it’s Levi’s brand so probably will last a long time.

LOVE THIS, SO MANY POCKETS! I am very petite and muscular (Height: 4ft 8in, Weight: 108lbs, Bust: 32D). I was trying to find a size that would fit my biceps & shoulders. First I ordered a Medium, but was way too big. So I exchanged for an X-Small. The denim has a slight stretch, but not much. It was perfect around the waist, but tight in the shoulder and arms. So I steamed it to relax the material and it ended up being a perfect fit! I can even layer w/ a light hoodie underneath.... Bonus Feature: I LOVE that there's so many pockets! (6 Total Pockets- 2 chest, 2 side, 2 inside) The inside pockets can fit an iPhone X with case.

Comfy; Large fits US size 10 These are not actually what I consider "jeggings". These are colored, fleece-lined leggings. I ordered the "navy blue- fleece-lined", and they could be mistaken for jeans from a distance, but not up close, since the material is usual leggings material in a blue color, with light stitching. They are very stretchy and comfortable, and pass the "squat test" (i.e. they are not see-through when I bend over). The back pockets are actual pockets, but the front ones are just for looks. As for sizing, I am 5 foot 7, weigh 165 pounds, have a short torso with a flat derriere and big stomach, and wear a US women's size 10 or 30 x 32 (medium leggings and pants at Old Navy and H&M). I ordered these leggings in a large since fleece-lined leggings sometimes run smaller, and they fit perfectly without slipping down or digging into my waist. They do not offer much tummy control, and the back pockets are placed a little low for my preference. On me, they are full length and not 7/8 (see my pics). Overall, I am happy with the warmth and comfort of these leggings, which I plan to wear all winter with long sweaters. I would definitely order them again.

Waist isn't best for curvy girls I wanted to love these because the denim color was perfect, and the fleece inside felt super-soft. Unfortunately, although these weren't tight in the legs, they had trouble accommodating my butt. I had the dreaded giant gap in the back of the waist band which is very uncomfortable. In the front, these didn't even come up to my belly button even though they do on the model. The waist was just very weird for this size (XXL). You might be able to reign in the waist with a belt to gather up the extra fabric and eliminate the gap in the back. If you're tall, be aware that these will be high-waters on you. I'm 5'8" and would not be able to wear these without looking silly. These have a real zipper and button in case you were curious, they aren't a pull-on pant. These were a "no" for me based on how they fit my body, but construction-wise they are high quality. I didn't see any loose threads, crooked stitching, or anything else. If you wear a smaller size and are 5'6" or shorter, these might work better for you.

Not for long legs. I was so excited to get these insulated jeans, just in time for cooler weather! The quality is nice, but the fit just doesn't work for me. (5' 10" 155 lbs. 30" inseam wearing a size L) Pros: Cozy, soft insulated jeans that look like any other pair of jeans. Color and stone washing add cute styling. They do fit fairly true to size, just not quite right for me. They feel like quality materials. Cons: They are too short! To be fair, most standard jeans are, but these feel extra short. They bunch funny around the knee. The fit at my waist, hips, bum looks like riding pants...too loose in the wrong places. I'm keeping the pants, I don't hate them, I will just wear high boots and long sweaters, sweatshirts or jackets to hide the akward parts and enjoy the warmth of these soft insulated jeans.

STYLE: - The waist has an adorable 3-button detail that is super-flattering and holds in any pudgy tummy if you ate too many carbs that day ;) - Easy to wear and match with everything - we all need a good pair of white pants. - These pants make you FEEL confident & sexy which actually MAKES you confident & sexy! - I don't know what it is about white pants but men LOVE when I wear these - especially with heels!!! Seriously, I get stared at to the point of awkward, sometimes. lol SIZING: - I usually buy Wax jeans, jeggings & shorts and the sizing is pretty consistent. Also, my weight fluctuates so I like that they use stretchy, forgiving material that hugs in a flattering way. - I'm 5'8", 34D, 150 lbs, and measure 38/31/41 and I usually go with size 13 unless I want a super tight fit, then size 11. - I have pretty muscular calves (15.5" circumference) and these pants, even in the larger of the 2 sizes I order: 13 vs 11, still hug very tightly to the calves which causes creasing & gathering at the knees - see pics). - The length is otherwise perfect. - Designed for a snug fit from waist to ankle, so don't size down. They'll be tight enough unless you have zero muscles and/or fat. FABRIC: - The fabric shrinks temporarily after washed and then stretches as you wear it, so keep that in mind. - The pockets show through fabric (in white anyway) but my undies don't show when I wear tan or beige. - Thong looks best with these pants, as granny-panties don't let the fabric & back seam do it's form-fitting thing to show off your ass ;) COMFORT: - Soft, movable fabric make these a very comfortable all-day jean. - Waist is a bit loose on me, so I end up having to play tug-of-war with them if I don't wear a belt, especially if I wear them for several hours (because they stretch & relax as you wear them - as mentioned above). Overall opinion: As a curvy chic, these are by far the best fitting brand and I will keep buying Wax jeans as long as available! So I suggest: Buy them and do so in a few colors. Make sure white is one of them but also get some in colors you are confident to wear when you're on your cycle (in a larger size if needed). They look HOT with pumps, knee boots, flip-flops, strappy flats and even barefeet! Don't forget to click so I know!

NOT MIDRISE First problem: the description says "mid-rise", yet the picture is of jeans that are CLEARLY LOW RISE. Honestly I was looking for low rise, but decided I would settle for mid-rise if that's what I received. Ordered a size 7 first (5'5", 135 lbs) which was WAY too small, plus the tags on what they sent me all said "High-rise". Returned those and ordered size 9 thinking the high-rise part was just a fluke. Nope. They sent me high-rise again (as can be seen in the picture) and on top of that, they are slightly loose in the waist, which means they would be falling down in no time. Sending these back for a refund. Won't be ordering again.

Cute jeans, surprisingly a little too long I've ordered another wax denim in a size 11 that fit well but these were a little too tight, might be because the other pair had more stretch and these weren't very stretchy (for reference, I'm 5'8", 160 lbs, 36D, 32" waist, 40" hips). The waist cut into me and gave me a muffin top and the legs gave me serious "stuffed sausage" look below my butt. The fabric is thick enough so my underwear didn't show through and seemed good quality. I worry about jeans being too short but these were actually a bit long on me causing the fabric to gather around my ankles which I didn't really mind. I love the 3 button detail and white jeans are a staple item so if you can get the sizing right, I recommend buying them.

A break from high-waisted, casual wear, may be too long for some … Sidefeel Women Casual Loose Mid Rise Cargo Jeans GREAT I can see my belly button. I can breathe. Not high-waisted. They sit more-so on my hips. While I don’t really want to go back to all 90s styles, I enjoy getting away from crazy tight/too high-waisted pants when I can! POCKETS The front pockets fit my iPhone SE. It’s a little awkward when I first sit down (the phone digs into my hip a little), but it’s not like the phone is going to fall out. I think I will eventually not notice it. The back pockets also fit my iPhone SE, but when I sit down it sticks out quite a bit and I am not sure my phone will stay secure. OKAY The material is heavier than I expected, but it is not uncomfortable. Without shoes on these hit the top of my feet/down to the back of my heel. They almost drag on the floor – almost but not quite. So, the length is okay for me. I am 5’8’’. I don’t know if it will be an issue for others. SIZING If I followed their size chart based on my measurements only, I could have potentially gone with a size 4 which lists “hip 37.4”. However, I chose to follow the suggestions of previous reviewers to size up to a size 6 which lists “hip 38.39”. I am happy with my size choice. For reference, I am 5'8" and 125 lbs, with an athletic build, and my current measurements are bust 35.5, 28.5 inches for my waist, and 37.5 inches for my hips. OVERALL There is nothing necessarily wrong with the style of these pants, but there is nothing spectacular either. They are definitely for casual wear – not crazy flattering or unflattering for that matter. So, I don’t not recommend them. They are high quality and a break from the norm – you’ll have to decide for yourself on the style.

Great pants, just not right for me So let me start off by saying that the first impression quality is great on these. There were a few loose threads in the zipper area, but most of those were easily pulled free and its no big deal. Moving on from that, I got the size 18 in grey twill. I usually buy 16A jeans. These jeans are very long. I admit I didnt pay attention to the inseam length as that isn't usually a problem for me being about average height (5' 5 or so). These are like 5 or 6 inches too long. See my pic, my feet are flat. I dont normally wear heels so that is an issue. Next, I think the fabric might almost be too stretchy. Or perhaps just a little too thin. I have quite a bit of a pudge in front of my stomach. Most of my jeans, even the super stretchy skinny jeans, even it out. These don't. And its very obvious. Finally these fit super well around the waist but got a bit too loose around the knees. Not much an issue but there you are. As for a huge plus! There's front pockets!🎉🎉 Im not sure if I'll return yet as they're not bad pants, just not quite right for me which is a shame because they're such a good price.

Short, not capris length At 5’7”tall these are more like clam digger length on me rather than capris length. I’m also not in love with the fit because while the waist fits me perfectly (for reference 127 lbs 27” waist ordered size 4) there’s some rather odd bunching going on in the hip, leg and butt area that’s not shown in the model photos and the fit is somewhat a little tighter in the calf area. Material wise, I got the dark blue and although the material is pretty nice, not too thin or too thick, I’m not really liking the color too much, makes them look kind of cheap and again, doesn’t look like the same color as shown on the model. The description also lists these as mid rise but they are actually high rise and hit me just slightly below my belly button, not necessarily a bad thing if you prefer high rise but if you’re looking for mid rise, keep looking. What I liked, good stretch (no need to size up - if anything size down one, especially if your measurements fall in between the sizes noted in the sizing guide) , nice deep front pockets and they are comfortable and seem pretty durable. Bottom line: 3 stars mainly because the listing description and seller photos are somewhat misleading. These jeans are just okay. I’ll still wear from time to time but for casual outings and outdoor activities only.

I think this dress would probably look best on thinner women with smaller breast, as the shape makes me look larger than I do in my other dresses. The color is nice and is a heavier jeans type material. I think I could probably make this dress work as a long open jacket, instead of buttoning it and wearing as a dress. As a dress though - this is definitely not for me.

Super Flattering and Comfortable Overalls If you're a toddler parent at this time then you know Halloween is all about dressing as Ms. Rachel. Since I could no longer fit into my pre-pregnancy overalls I love so much and got in Europe so can never replace, I went to Amazon for some "cheap" overalls to throw on for Halloween. I'm 5' 6" and 150lbs give or take a few lbs and I ordered the Medium and these fit perfectly! Not only do they fit perfectly but they're pretty darn flattering, for overalls, lol. The material is not thin, it looks and feels like I spent a good amount of money on them so I was pleasantly surprised. I had a few moms asking where I got them so from other perspectives they must be nice. I definitely feel like these are more durable than my favorite pair. They're not thick in the way that woukd limit motion, they're thick as in they don't feel like they'll tear when bending and moving around but they're not hot or stiff. I would definitely and have already recommended these to others.

SUPER CUTE AND COMFY 10/10 recommend🤌🏼 These overalls were comfy and cute! I got so many compliments on my outfit and these overalls. Im 5’1 130lbs and got a medium. They were definitely on the bigger side (which I wanted). So just keep that in mind if you are looking for more form fitting or the oversized look. The material is also on the thinner side for overalls and they have a decent amount of stretch without actually being stretchy material. Didn’t have any issues with quality or faulty hardware on the overalls (tested this out thoroughly while being a little festival gremlin). Also for the girlies who feel like these might not look good with their body shape, im shaped like a freakin apple and they still looked cute👏🏼

I’m happy with these! I’ve tried SO MANY overalls and these are pretty great! I’m tall, (5’9” and 160lbs) so I wish they were a tad longer but overall they’re perfect. Soft, a little stretchy, didn’t shrink in the wash, true to size. I’ll be ordering more in different colors

Loved it, until I washed it When it arrived, the material was soft, like well worn denim. The fit was great, just roomy enough, not so tight that you can’t bend over. Quite a bit too long, but at 5’2”, I’m used to that. (The picture is before washing.) Then I washed them. Instructions said ‘cold water, do NOT tumble dry’, so that’s what I did. Yikes. I’d say they shrank somewhere between 1/2 to a full size. They ended up extremely wrinkled and the material became super stiff and rough, like the denim my Grandpa used to wear. I’m going to continue to dye them to keep them from getting too faded! UPDATED REVIEW: After having them for a little while, I wanted to add some notes. They are soo comfortable and I find myself wanting to wear them all the time. However, the pockets have Never laid quite right and this can really affect the way they look on. The front pocket bunches and leaves big gaps between my body and the overalls. The side pockets don’t want to fit correctly in their slots because due to the buttons pulling on them on each side, and the buttons aren’t even functional. I wash these by hand and hang them to dry, and even after steaming them, they still don’t look the way I want them to or as they are in the model photos. They will be great in spring and autumn with a cardigan or sweatshirt. I recommend.

WEDGIE "SHUT UP" WASH SIZE COMPARISON Because I do not usually spend this kind of money on clothing and am pretty unreliable with completing online returns, I read every review I could find on multiple websites before I selected my size. I still got it wrong (lol). I don’t usually leave reviews, but there is a LOT of conflicting information about the sizing of these jeans out there and I want to share what I found to be true.When selecting your size, I recommend taking two factors into consideration:1. What is your body shape/type – i.e. where do you carry your weight? If you are someone with more of an hourglass shape or more thick in the thighs/butt, I would follow the guidance of most of the reviews and size up. If you are someone like me with a small/medium butt and legs, I would recommend selecting either your normal size, or one size down.2. How do you want the jeans to fit – i.e. do you want a relaxed fit or a tighter fit? For reference, I am around 5’7, 122 pounds, and usually a size 26/size 2 in pants. The size 26 Wedgies fit me perfectly right out of the bag. However, based on other reviewers’ experience, 100% cotton jeans will relax/mold to your body over time and use. If I kept the size 26, they would have evolved to more of a “boyfriend” style relaxed fit on me. I personally wanted a snugger fit. I then ordered the size 25. They were tight on me and not the most comfortable right out of the bag (they also made my butt look smaller which was the opposite of what I was hoping). However, after wearing them for a few hours, especially sitting in them, they relaxed and fit me exactly the way I envisioned.I attached pictures of me in both and indicated the size I am wearing in each photo. This is what each pair looked like immediately after opening the package - I did not take a photo of the size 25 after they relaxed, but trust me when I say, they're worth it! Hope this helps someone!

HOLY BUTT-GRAIL JEANS I’ve had a vision of what I want my jeans to do and look like on me and I finally found them! They’re PERFECT! So perfect that I can’t stop sneaking a peek at my butt any time I walk past a mirror in these lol. I am 5’5”, about 125 lbs, waist measures 27”, hips/butt measure 39”, and these are a size 26 in Tango Light. Before Levi’s, my go to jeans were American Eagle size 2 for a snug fit or 4 for a comfortable relaxed fit. According to the Levi’s size chart, my measurements put me in size 26 for my waist and size 27 for my hips. I have a pair of originals in size 27 because they are 100% cotton and I was afraid buying to fit my waist would flatten my butt, but they have relaxed really nicely to more of a boyfriend look. Seeing how well the 100% cotton originals stretched, I felt confident buying the Wedgies in size 26 and I’m glad I did. They *feel* like 100% cotton, they hold in and support like 100% cotton, but that 1% of elastane makes bending over and bending at the knee so much more comfortable, and they fit beautifully. A tiny bit of butt flattening at first, but once I wore them around the house for an hour they were perfect!

Cute, but uncomfortable. I'm really torn on these jeans. While they look cute on my butt, I found them to be pretty uncomfortable and don't love the price. The button fly paired with the stiffer denim, plus the fact that these were probably a size too small on me, made them kind of miserable to wear. For reference, I'm currently anywhere from an 8 to a 12 in jeans, depending on the brand/fit/material, and my measurements are currently 44"-32"-44". I'm 5'3" with a larger butt and thighs, with a short torso and longer legs. I usually buy regular length pants because short tends to be just a tad too short for my taste, but still too long to be that perfect ankle length... Hopefully that makes sense. I got these in a SIZE 30 in the color TANGO LIGHT. As you can see in the photos, the color is lovely but they're a bit tight on me. I suspected I may need a 31 but hoped they would relax a little after a few hours of wear and become the perfect snug fit in the 30. While that may be the case, I honestly don't know if I like them enough to be miserable for the hours it'll take to make that happen. I will say that the Premium material is a bit softer than the other stiffer Levi's jeans, like the standard Wedgie Skinnies. They still aren't soft though, so if that's what you're looking for, I would say these aren't for you. I'd recommend these for someone looking for a sturdier denim with little to no give. I'm sure they will last a long time. I ended up returning these and am unsure if I'll ever bother to try a 31. I've decided I'm not really a lover of the button fly. Oh well.

For reference, I'm 5'6", 145 lbs, with a 28" waist and 38" hips, and the Medium fit perfectly. The fabric is made of a nicer denim material than I expected, and I love that they have a bit of stretch to them. It makes them so comfortable. I feel like these jeans look great with so many different style tops. I got the dark wash and it looks even better than the pictures. I didn't like them at first, seemed too baggy,, BUT..then I Fell in Love with them.. I got an XL .. After I washed them they shrunk up enough in the back and the crotch wasn't so long anymore,,. AND it DIDN'T seem to shrink in the Waistline At ALL..( I was worried because I washed/dried HOT ). SOO NOW I LOVE THEM!!! Very stretchy /Soft..they are a looser fit, but I just wear them with a long sweater/Tshirt and the length is perfect for me, right above my ankle ( I'm 5'10 ).. as you wear them throughout the day I noticed they started stretching more around the waist, but not so much you feel like they'll fall down..LOL..Also They had NO CHEMICAL Smell At ALL..
