Flattering!! (sorry for the blurry mirror) For reference I’m 5’4” 135lbs and a 31 year old mom. I ordered these first in size 4/27 (my normal Levi’s size) but they were too big. I re-ordered in size 2/26 and they fit perfectly. I am normally not a size 2/26 so they ran large on me. They are extremely flattering on my pear shape. NO gap in the back of the waist! I have a thick bottom half (and short legs) and I don’t like to wear short shorts. These fit really nice! Nice mid to high rise on them. You can see in my picture it comes to about my belly button. You don’t have to worry about bending over and showing plumbers butt. The dark wash is really nice and classy looking. The pocket placement is great. They are overall extremely flattering. A little bit of stretch, but not too much. Pictures taken when the shorts were new but they’ve been washed and worn since with no issues to the actual shorts. My only issue was with the denim wash. I did have to use a color catcher while washing. It still dyed one of my white hand towels light blue! Be careful of color transfer with the dark wash! The only thing I wish was the bottom leg holes of the shorts was a tiny bit smaller so it hugged my knees a little tighter. I’m just picky! The bottom is not sewn into place so you have to roll the shorts when you get them out of the dryer. They are really flattering on the backside and I definitely recommend them for pear shapes like me. I love not having to pull shorts down from riding up when I walk. That is why I like bike shorts and Bermuda shorts. If you have big thighs you know what I mean. I think curvy girls in particular will love these. The quality on mine is very good! I am definitely going to stock up and order a bunch for summer. I wish they had more colors in my size. Hopefully this review was to someone out there!

Too long for petites. SIZE DOWN (stretches out) I really liked these jeans until I started wearing them all day. They stretch out SO MUCH, and the waistband is much lower than I expected for a high-rise jean. I’m short so I knew the split hem would be too long, but rolled up they look great. Since it’s a raw hem, I might just cut them off. I’m 5’0”, 148lbs, and normally wear an 8 short in GAP or Abercrombie jeans. I ordered an 8 and they’re actually too big! They’re best for curvy figures; roomy in the waist, hips, crotch and butt. Plenty of stretch and wiggle room. I’m afraid if I sized down, they’d be too snug in the thighs. The fabric is really soft and feels similar to Abercrombie or American Eagle denim, but unfortunately the waistband feels a little scratchy and uncomfortable. Another issue is the back pocket placement. Instead of lifting my bigger booty up, they tend to pull it down and flatten it out. The back pockets are super low! Size down if you’re between sizes, or size up for a looser fit. Hope this helps! 🙂

12% polyester These run pretty true to size - maybe ever-so-slightly small. I ordered my usual size medium, and they fit tightly but comfortable. I did not put them in the dryer, just in case, I didn't want any shrinkage. The label also says to hang dry, but they are cotton 88% and polyester 12%, so I don't see why you couldn't dry these. Speaking of the material, I am disappointed that they have any polyester since the listing claims 100% cotton. My skin has an aversion to polyester, so I try to keep it out of my wardrobe. These feel decently soft on my skin, so I am hoping they will be ok, but I am taking off 1 star due to the claim of 100% cotton, since that is not what the label says. Other than the material, these are just like the like the listing photos. They are baggy but a flattering cut. I am 5'3" and these come below my ankles, but they are not too long. I'm excited to put together some artsy outfits with these!

I have absolutely nothing negative to say about it. I saw these Sweezarmo denim stretch joggers with an elasticized drawstring waistband and ordered them in my usual size small after consulting the company’s provided size chart. The Sky Blue color is represented accurately in the description photos, and the size runs true to the chart measurements. In person, the fabric looks like a true pair of denim jeans and to touch, feels like regular denim….with a little stretch. I was surprised at how much stretch they have in them. The weight of the denim is even close to what I’d expect for a pair of summer jeans. Prior to receiving them, I had some was concern about the waist having enough stretch, since my waist measurement is at least a couple inches larger than the posted measurement on the size chart. Sometimes elasticized waistbands can be constricting without much stretch, but that’s not the case with these. For those with a smaller waist, the drawstring is functional and can be cinched up for the right fit. On me, I feel the pants make my hips look big…but maybe some of that’s due to me being fuller in the waist!?! One feature I absolutely love about the pants is the pockets. Two in the front and two on the back. The front pockets are nice and deep for slipping hands or a cell phone in. I feel the pants are comfortable to wear and on the right figure (i.e. models in the listing photos) they look adorable! The quality of these is good from the materials used to the craftsmanship in the stitching. The pants are actually long enough that I’d be comfortable wearing them with sandals or flats without cuffing them, or rolling them up once or twice for an ankle or cropped look. Due to the thickness of the denim fabric, the elasticized waistband is a little bulky looking and feeling, so I think cropped tops or tops tucked in look the best with these bottoms. Overall I’m pleased with the denim pants (I personally don’t refer to them as joggers). They’re comfortable and wash up well, following care instructions. I will get wear out of them, but they won’t be bottoms I wear out and about often. They are so cute, but just not flattering on my body build. I appreciate the opportunity to try these, but in seeing how they look on me, I probably wouldn’t order them again for the price. But, for others that are considering them, I would recommend them for their quality and suggest staying true to size. For reference, my measurements are: Height - 5’4” Bust - 35” Waist - 30” Arm length - 22.5” Shoulder width - 15” Weight - 120# Inseam - 31”

COMFORTABLE, FLATTERING JEANS AT A GREAT PRICE I am 5'4" and weigh 173 lbs and purchased the medium and they fit great; just the way that I like them. The ad says they are bell bottom, which they are clearly not; and for that, I am grateful for. I almost didn't buy them based on that description; I am not a fan of bell bottoms. The length was perfect for me, but I can see ladies 5'6" and up would probably prefer them a little longer. These jeans have a great amount of stretch and look so flattering on a curvier figure, however they're so forgiving, they would look great on any figure. I would LOVE it if this style pant came in white or other color options; I'd buy them in a heartbeat. The price for these jeans at the time of this review is $29.99 with 20% off. That's a GREAT price. I paid $50 for a different brand that fit similar to these; but I actually prefer this brand. I totally recommend these jeans.

Short but mighty Sizing info first off: I'm 5'1" and weigh 125. I usually wear a 2 or a 4, or a Small, in bottoms. I ordered these overalls in a S and that seems to have been about right. I could probably fit into an XS to get it looking less baggy at the waist, but wouldn't get the overall slumpy look I was going for then. With that out of the way, let's go over some cons: my main complaint is that these are much shorter than I anticipated them being. That's not to say they're obscenely short or anything, but when I sit down I feel like these are Very Short. I'd guess these have maybe a 4" inseam? A second con is that the straps are...flimsy isn't quite the right word, as the fabric appears to be of a good thickness, but the length sliders don't move easily, and when they do, the fabric of the straps folds and clumps up, making adjusting the straps even harder. Now, some pros: the fabric seems to be real denim, not a polyester imitation, and has a good thickness to it. The stitching on the folded leg hems seems strong and more than adequate. These run true to size and the color I received matches the color in the listing. The hip pockets are real pockets, big enough to fit my phone, which in this day and age of fake pockets, is a big deal to me. Overall, if you don't mind a short inseam and somewhat annoying straps, these are a solid buy. I anticipate them lasting well.

Although I’m tempted to go outside my comfort zone and possibly try the light wash.... ****Original review: I’m 5’4” 135ish and I ordered size 6. I range between a size 4-6 or 27-28. I don’t like my shorts very tight fitting. Unless you are not curvy or very slim, then you may not need to go with your larger size. Or maybe you want a super tight fitting look, in that case you could probably stick with your normal size. They are extremely comfortable and flattering especially in the dark wash. I am a 30 year old toddler mom and these are very cute and practical for me! I don’t like short shorts so these are a win win. I don’t have to worry about them riding up and pulling them down during the day. Ladies who have thick thighs that rub, a big bottom and/or mommy tummy these are for you! I’m so glad to have found some comfy and cute longer length shorts that look flattering. They have a nice stretch and they don’t give me muffin top or cut into my stomach. I think curvy and/or plus size girls will love these too! I ordered like 5 pairs because I love them so much lol! The only slight issue I had was they stretched out a little bit when I wore them, but it wasn’t a big issue. They weren’t totally bagging out or anything. The only other thing would be I wish the back pockets were blank with no wavy line, not a big deal, just personal preference. Overall they just fit my daily casual mom style. I like how the hem is finished with a little roll, makes them look more pulled together in my opinion. They look cute with tanks, tees or even long sleeves! I like how they have a skinny leg, otherwise Bermuda shorts tend to look frumpy on me if they aren’t fitted to the leg. Levi’s always comes through for me. These definitely look cute and classy and more expensive then the price tag! If you hate short shorts you need these! They are a total confidence booster for those of us who want to hide cellulite, big thighs or just prefer a little more coverage. No inner thighs sticking together in the heat with the longer length lol! Lastly, you can’t beat the price!!

I’m normally a 4 in jeans. Size 6 is comfortable, ever so slightly loose. The size 4 fits as well (I tried both) but I just preferred the size 6 because it had a little more wiggle room. The pictures of me posted in the shorts are of the size 6. A lot of the reviews said to go up a size and I can see why because the size 4 was pretty fitted on me. I don’t like my shorts very tight fitting. Unless you are not curvy or very slim, then you may not need to go with your larger size. Or maybe you want a super tight fitting look, in that case you could probably stick with your normal size. They are extremely comfortable and flattering especially in the dark wash. I am a 30 year old toddler mom and these are very cute and practical for me! I don’t like short shorts so these are a win win. I don’t have to worry about them riding up and pulling them down during the day. Ladies who have thick thighs that rub, a big bottom and/or mommy tummy these are for you! I’m so glad to have found some comfy and cute longer length shorts that look flattering. They have a nice stretch and they don’t give me muffin top or cut into my stomach. I think curvy and/or plus size girls will love these too! I ordered like 5 pairs because I love them so much lol! The only slight issue I had was they stretched out a little bit when I wore them, but it wasn’t a big issue. They weren’t totally bagging out or anything. The only other thing would be I wish the back pockets were blank with no wavy line, not a big deal, just personal preference. Overall they just fit my daily casual mom style. I like how the hem is finished with a little roll, makes them look more pulled together in my opinion. They look cute with tanks, tees or even long sleeves! I like how they have a skinny leg, otherwise Bermuda shorts tend to look frumpy on me if they aren’t fitted to the leg. Levi’s always comes through for me. These definitely look cute and classy and more expensive then the price tag! If you hate short shorts you need these! They are a total confidence booster for those of us who want to hide cellulite, big thighs or just prefer a little more coverage. No inner thighs sticking together in the heat with the longer length lol! Lastly, you can’t beat the price!!

Cute Style, Flattering Cut, Comfortable Stretch, Sturdy Denim I ordered these jeans in color Blue, size 8. They fit me just the same as most name brand jeans of that size fit me. The waist is high yet well-proportioned in that it doesn't rise above my belly button like some other high-waisted pants I've purchased. I like where the hemline hits my legs. For reference, I'm 5'4". The fabric is substantial in weight and has the feel of a very comfortable, stretchy cotton denim. I've put these jeans through 5 laundry cycles using cold water and one brief drying cycle on medium heat and although they did shrink a bit, it didn't affect the fit much because the fabric is stretchy. I think the cut of these jeans is both stylish and flattering, and the wide pant legs allow lots of freedom of motion in the thigh area. There was one hiccup, however. The jeans arrived carrying a strong odor in the fabric, which to me smells like petroleum. This is the reason I took off one star. I've now put them through 5 wash cycles, one brief dryer cycle and hung them outside in the sunshine. I'd researched that baking soda, vinegar and/or lemon juice might help, so I used those in the last wash cycle and it did help to a degree.

Nice thick denim These are super comfortable pants. I did get the extra large and there are a little bit on the big side. I'm not a pregnant individual. I am 5'7, 190 lb. So, I'm a chunky lady. So, I thought a pair of pants like this would give me that little extra around the top. I wish I would have ordered the L instead of the XL. It's a little big around the legs and a little bit loose on the waist. But, the length is good and the quality is there. They're a nice boot cut so you can wear them with pretty much any kind of shoe . These are nice jeans. I'm still going to wear them even though they're a little bit loose and I'll just add a belt. It'll look great with a sweater. I like the fact that they are thicker and we're going into a season that's cold. They might be a little heavy for summer time. But all in all, they are really nice jeans. If you have a bigger tummy or you're pregnant it'll be nice because you've got that little extra elastic on the sides. I pulled it out the top to see how far it would go and it really had a lot of give.

Shorter Inseam, Overall Skinny Fit These seem well suitable for 1st and 2nd trimester for most people, I think. They are a little firmer elastic than I expected so I think it will depend on the person since some people gain more inches on their waist during a pregnancy than others. As far as I can tell, I think these will comfortably come out as far as 13 inches past your pre-pregnancy waist, and maybe a few inches more, depending on your tolerance towards tighter elastic feeling. However I think these are really great for apple-shaped people or who suffer from extreme bloating. They hit right on the natural waist and with a longer top that covers the elastic, they look just like fashionable jeans. Actually these are probably the most flattering jeans I've ever worn, they make my legs look soooo skinny. I usually buy jeans in size 10 - and these in size Small are like a size 6 on the legs part and like from size 8 out to like a good 22 or so for the waist. The unstretched waist is 30 inches, the rise is 9.5", the inseam is actually 27" (and I am 5'3" so this is perfect for me). Unstretched hip is 36", they stretch out fine to my 38" hips. My waist size fluctuates a lot and then it's not unusual for me to bloat out as much as 10 inches by dinner so these are so great for me. I'll see how they hold up to more washings but I'm probably going to try out more from this brand. The only cons I really see so far is the lack of front pockets and that the inside thread feels a bit scratchy in places.

The unstretched waist is 30 inches, the rise is 9.5", the inseam is actually 27" (and I am 5'3" so this is perfect for me). Unstretched hip is 36", they stretch out fine to my 38" hips. My waist size fluctuates a lot and then it's not unusual for me to bloat out as much as 10 inches by dinner so these are so great for me. I'll see how they hold up to more washings but I'm probably going to try out more from this brand. The only cons I really see so far is the lack of front pockets and that the inside thread feels a bit scratchy in places.

Pattern makes up for deficits These are cute and I'll definitely wear them often. They're a tiny bit big but I anticipated that since there was no size 0 (5'6" ~115 25/26 inch waist). The waist fits okay, but my waist is thicker for my frame. The thighs are a little baggy, but that's my life. I have very narrow legs. I feel like if they were not patterned the slightly off fit might look sloppier, but since they are I'm fine with it. I have trouble with jeans being too tight in the waist if they fit elsewhere, so these are actually more comfortable for me. For reference, I have bought the smallest size jeans available to accommodate my little booty and legs but feel absolutely unwell from how tight they are in the waist (specifically mostly cotton madewell jeans in a 23). The button is a little cheap/loose as others have mentioned on this brands other listings. I don't think it will be an issue for me, but if they fit my waist more snuggly it would be. I'm 5'6" and they're perfect length for tennis shoes, but if you're any shorter you'll need heels. They have a frayed hem, but there is a hem stitch above it. So you could cut it, but I don't think it would look the same. These are definitely stretchy jeans. Wish they had a slightly higher cotton content because I fear baggy knees in my future, but like I said, the pattern helps them look sleeker than they are. I got lots of compliments. Decent value and free people dupe.

Cute But Sizing Chart is Off, Read Description for Correct Size These are some unique and cute jeans that can be dressed up or down. And they are long leg friendly!! Yes! It's hard finding pants long enough for my 32.5" inseam. I can actually wear low heels with these jeans. I ordered a Size 6 according to the sizing chart in the photos but these pants are too big. I usually wear a size 2 or 4. I am 5'10, 123 lbs, 32B-26-35 for reference. So with those measurements a size 6 should fit but these jeans are too big in the waist, butt and hip area. If you read the product description, the sizing they list there is probably more correct as they list a Size 2 with a 26" waist and 34" hips. Wish I would have read this instead of using the sizing chart in the photos. Also these are very high waisted. I have a really high waist, basically have no torso, all legs and in order for me not to have a droopy looking crotch area, I had to pull these up really high. Luckily, I know how to alter clothing to fit me the way I like so I will wash them first to see if there's any shrinkage and then take the pants in as needed.

Check, Please! These green and white checkered pants are adorable, comfortable and unique. Even though they're available on Amazon, you're unlikely to end up in a room with anyone else wearing them. The denim is on the thinner side if you compare them with typical jeans (Levis, etc.) but they're not too lightweight to wear in average NYC fall temperatures. They're a slightly stretchy, medium-wight twill. I ordered a 6 based on the size chart but should have listened to Amazon's advice when they said to get a 4. If Amazon makes a recommendation, I would follow it over the manufacturer's size chart in this instance. For reference, I'm 5'8", 110 lbs, 26" waist, 35" hip, 33" inseam. I do like the length, although according to the size chart it's the same for the 4 and 6. It's a 32" inseam according to my measurement but feels longer. I'm not accounting for the rise, which is average. I expected it to be shorter as I assumed these were going to be cut like junior's pants, but to my relief they're not. Maybe I'll wash them in warm water and tumble dry to tighten them up a bit. They're 98% cotton so they should shrink somewhat if you want them to. However, the green dye is already running into the white so it might be best to use cold water. I love the flare—they're as close to bell bottoms as I've seen in this century. They're wider than a boot cut unless the footwear in question is moon boots. They're unhemmed and have fashionably frayed ends but there's a line of subtle stitches 1/4" above the bottom to keep the fabric from further fraying. There are five functional pockets in all: two traditional jean pockets on the rump and two stitched round pockets in front, plus one small coin pocket, and there are belt loops that can accommodate a range of belt widths. For such an unusual, comfortable pair of jeans $39.99 seems reasonable if you like the way they fit. Again, my only concern is that the green dye will bleed into the white.

True to size These pants are very high waisted, have decent pockets for female pants with a frayed, wide flare leg. The denim has a touch of elastic to it so they hug well without needing to do gymnastics to get them on. I was impressed that not only were they long enough but at 5’10”, touched the floor barefooted, something most pants never do. The zipper is metal and well sewn in, the rivets are all well pressed but the button is just a snap on which means it’s loose on the fabric and will not last overly long. They wash well without shrinking, keeping their vivid white and black pattern but the frayed bottom did fray a lot more. May need either a stitch line or some fray stop to keep them from falling apart long term.

Not the product in the picture For reference, I am 5’6” 142 lbs with a rectangular torso and thick thighs, I ordered a medium, as suggested, and I don’t have any complaints about the way they fit. They are snug on my body type, because I don’t have much of a waist, but if you are on the skinny side or have an hourglass figure, i’m sure it would be different. There’s nothing overtly wrong with the shorts, but they simply aren’t what I ordered. I included a picture of the color and style for reference, next to what they look like on. I went back and double checked what I ordered, thinking maybe there were two grays and I clicked on the wrong one - but I ordered correctly and didn’t see another one that looks like what I got that it could have been mixed up with. The shorts in the photo look like they’re made of soft, swing-y material, almost like pajamas. The fabric of the shorts I received is stiff and rough. It’s also almost a blue, and not at all the neutral gray pictured. They don’t lie flat against your body, but puff out. I’ve tried on worse shorts, and can always use these for yard work, but I don’t think I’d have looked twice at the real ones vs what I liked about the ones advertised. They aren’t sheer, they have good back coverage. I don’t understand the super heavy-duty waistband paired with an unnecessarily long and superfluous drawstring (it literally does nothing), but I guess it’s not hurting anything. Not worth the hassle of sending them back at this price point, but beware you’re not getting what you think you are.

It’s also almost a blue, and not at all the neutral gray pictured. They don’t lie flat against your body, but puff out. I’ve tried on worse shorts, and can always use these for yard work, but I don’t think I’d have looked twice at the real ones vs what I liked about the ones advertised. They aren’t sheer, they have good back coverage. I don’t understand the super heavy-duty waistband paired with an unnecessarily long and superfluous drawstring (it literally does nothing), but I guess it’s not hurting anything. Not worth the hassle of sending them back at this price point, but beware you’re not getting what you think you are.

Cute!! Needs a size chart Okay, so I actually really love these! However, one star deduction for not having a size chart anywhere—that drives me crazy, since clothes can vary so much within the S,M,L etc. sizing. I am usually between a small/medium in pants, size 28 roughly because I’m newly postpartum. Anyhow, I opted to get the medium because I didn’t want it to be too tight in the waist. Well… I could’ve gotten the small probably, and would’ve preferred those as I’m still losing weight. The medium DOES work for me still, it’s just a bit bigger and baggier. I think it does work with the current style of looser jeans, so I can make it work. However, as I said, a small might’ve been a bit better fitting. There isn’t a whole lot of stretch, maybe the teeny tiniest bit in the waist, but not enough to be labeled stretchy pants by any means. The makeup is 75% cotton, and the rest was viscose and polyester. They feel like medium weight denim, fairly good quality. I was actually super impressed with the color/pattern. Sometimes leopard patterns can look so incredibly cheap if not done correctly. These do not, in my opinion. They can be dressed up or down easily. Also, I am 5’7” and they go to about my ankle, a great length in my opinion. They are high waisted as well. The barrel look was the perfect amount, not overly barrel-y, but enough to clearly be barrel jeans. I found them to look quite flattering on me. Overall they felt quite comfortable! I am happy with them, I just wish I had gotten the small, but honestly I might buy the small because I like these enough to have a second pair! They also came way faster than I expected. It said it would take several weeks but came within about a week and half, so I’m super happy with that as well! Now if they could just get a size chart and actual product photos, I think these would sell out.

Nice fit, very comfortable These jeans might be the most comfortable jeans in my closet. I'm typically a small, with narrow hips and for me the fit around the waist and hips is perfect. They are stretchy so they will also work for someone with wider hips. The legs are consistently wide from the top of the thighs to the ankles. In my opinion, there are 2 things that could make these jeans really special from a style perspective. One is adding a little more width to the legs and the other is to make them slightly longer. I'm 5'4" and they are the perfect length, but with no room to shrink up. They will be pretty short on anyone 5'5" and over. But, some love that style so it's a preference thing I guess. The added width would give them a more unique look. But, they are great, everyday jeans that I'll wear during the day to run errands or to go hang at a friend's house. When I get home, they are so comfortable I don't feel compelled to rip them off and throw on yoga pants, like some other jeans I have. So, overall, I'm very happy with them, but wouldn't put them in "my favorites" category from a style perspective.

I like them, but they run a little large for Hudson Nicos As an elder millennial, I'm super stuck on the skinny jeans trend and having trouble getting into the straight leg style that everyone is into right now. However, I'm a huge fan of Hudson jeans, specifically the Nico style, so I was really excited to try these. And I do like them more than expected with a few caveats. They definitely run a little big when compared to other Nico jeans I have. I have several pairs of Nico Super Skinny jeans in size 25 that fit perfectly, so that's what I got these pants in, too. And while they fit OK, they're definitely large in the waist and I think I could have comfortably sized down. I'm 5'3, 100 lbs for reference. As you can probably see from my photos and the listing photos, the jeans are also quite a bit longer on me than on the model. Just a couple things to keep in mind when you're trying to decide what size to purchase! I'm still trying to feel totally comfortable in a straight leg jean, but these are helping me get there- they're super comfortable and relaxed (much more so than skinny jeans!) and they feel like a high-quality jean. If you're a fan of Hudson and looking for a good straight leg jean, I definitely recommend these... But you may want to size down from your other Hudsons!

I have hips and long legs so the medium is a little tight in the hips (but still wearable) and a little too short in the length as these are boot cut so they’re supposed to hang longer. I actually might have needed a size UP!!! I cut the hem out of the bottom to try to gain some length but they’re still slightly too short for my liking. The other thing that pissed me off is that I bought them for $19.99 and then the next day they were $14.99. I’d say they’re worth $14.99 but had I tried them on in a store I wouldn’t have bought them for $20.

Very Comfortable I kept eyeing this jumpsuit and was excited to see it went on sale so I bought it right away. I’m 5’7” about 220lbs and 40D and carry most of my weight in my belly. I WFH so it will certainly be one of my go to outfits during the week. The top area is more comfortable than I thought it would be considering it’s a scrunchy material. I’m not wearing a bra in the photos so I’d say you don’t really need one but I do recommend wearing nipple covers only because the scrunch material irritates that area. If you are wearing it for a short period of time then you can probably bypass the nip covers. At my height, the crotch is at the perfect area - not too short and not too long. The pants are an inch or two too long but I plan on mostly wearing this around the house but a platform sandal will fix that for you. The mustard color is more of a burnt orange than mustard. Doesn’t look like the color advertised but I’m completely in love and will be buying it again in black.

Size discrepancy between colors. Otherwise great skirt I bought the skirt in blue gradient and also in green, both in the large sizes. The large blue gradient one fits like a US 14, and the large green one fits like a 12. I am 5'7 approximately 205 lb, and the large blue gradient was snug but very flattering. That means that I, unfortunately, will return the large green skirt, which is probably 1-1/2 to 2 inches smaller in circumference. I'm not sure if all green skirts are off in sizing (or of the blue is off) or if it's just this one, but it was a disappointment nonetheless. Otherwise, the fabric is medium weight (i.e. won't show off every texture point from your body underneath) and has some stretch-- very flattering. The line from waist to hip will accommodate someone with a small waist and curves. The waist sits at the true waist and is not a drop waist garment.

Well made, comfortable, versatile skirt This denim skirt is a classic design, with a fly front, button. belt loops and pockets. The waistline has elastic in the back. The skirt is a nice weight and came out of the package ready to wear. It is a nice even blue and fairly soft. Too wide in hips and waist My daughter and I were debating whether to order these in my daughter’s regular size small or go down a size and order x-small. She is 5’5” 115 lbs and wears size small in jeans. According to the included chart these numbers put her in size small. However, her hip and waist measurements put her in size x-small according to the chart. Ultimately we decided to get size small because we have a couple of other pairs of jeans from this brand and size small in those fits her great. Unfortunately, these pants are too big for her in the waist and the hips. In the pictures I took the frontal view looks great but make sure to look at the back view picture too, so you can see how my daughter tightened them with a shoe lace to make them wearable. The fabric content according to the tag is 65% cotton 20% polyester 15% spandex. I’m not a fabric specialist by any means but 15% spandex? I have never seen jeans with such a high spandex content. And wouldn’t that make them very, very stretchy? These jeans however are not very stretchy. They are definitely not all cotton because they are soft for denim and have some give but I would not describe them as stretchy denim. All in all, the style is trendy, the workmanship is good, but size wise I wish they were running more true to the standard size small jeans.

Modified Mom Jeans - Black I am 5’5” and weigh 127 lbs. I bought the size 8 in black which fit true to size. At 72 years of age I wasn’t sure if this style would be flattering on me but decided to try them anyway. The last time I wore “mom jeans” was in the 1970’s! I didn’t notice any offensive odor like some of the other people noted in their reviews. They fit snug but will need to be hemmed up about 1.5”. I have not removed the tags or washed them yet but I don’t believe they will shrink. They have plenty of sideways horizontal stretch and they sit right at the waistline. I would buy these again, maybe in a darker blue jean color.

Love these Jeans! I love these jeans. They are a great fit, super comfortable. Not constricting, easy to move in. They are stretch denim. I ordered size 10. I am 5’3” tall 150 pounds, 41-31-41. These jeans have just enough stretch so that I could go down a size. They are very long. I am wearing 4 inch heels in the pictures. If I decide to hem the jeans it should not be too difficult as the hem is a 1 inch hem sewn all around the leg. They are supposed to have tummy control. I don’t think they do. To be tummy control they need a control panel sewn from the pockets to the zipper. Other than this oversight, they are terrific jeans, I love the style, the softness of the fabric and the high waist with wide, but not baggy legs.I took off one star because they are so long and the missing panel in the tummy area.

Fine, but not my favorite pants. Usually I don't go for high wasted pants; these definitely have some mom-jeans energy, but that's more of a style preference thing. They do fit fine, and button over my belly button. I'm 5'7", 135 lbs, and wear size sm/size 4. They are comfortable and don't pinch anywhere. They're a bit stinky. I washed them with like-colors, and they definitely lost some of their color, and still carry their manufacturing odor. It's no worse than the smell of new Levi's, and I'm hoping it'll be gone after a few more washes. I'm a bit sensitive to that kind of thing. The fabric is comfy. It feels like real jeans, but has a lot of elasticity which makes movement really easy. They're a bit plain for my usual style, but aside from the odor, they seem to be well-made, and a reasonable style. If you love the high-waisted look, I think you'll be pleased with these. They're a reasonable value, and very comfortable.

LOVE THESE JEANS I'm 5'2", 127 lbs. athletic build and got size 8. The 6 would have been good too but I like a looser fit. These jeans are GREAT, and are the first high waisted jeans I've liked (other high waists I've tried are stretchy through the leg, but the waist is too tight). The moment I slipped them on, you can feel that they are mostly cotton with enough viscose/spandex stretch. They got the right combination of materials here. Because of my height, they are longer but I wear them with 3 inch and up heel sandals and they make my legs look nice and long. I was able to kick off my heels and play with my daughter at two separate picnics and the stretch and fit makes them super comfortable. I'm thinking of what other color to buy in size 6, for those days I'm walking around with just my partner.

SO BEAUTIFUL!!! I LOVE these pants! They are flattering, stretchy and comfortable. I've been using them for a few weeks now and they're intact after washing them a few times. I love that they're straight down and the length is great, they cover my shoes. I've wore them with bodysuits, crop tops and t-shirts, and they look very beautiful. They are also true to size. I am 5' 4'' and around 130 pounds, usually wear size small, I ordered these in size 2 and they fit perfect, but also got them in size 4 and also fit great. A size more has been great particularly for those days of the month when I am a little bloated.

The jeans are a great quality super thick, beautiful color, but also super tight. Maybe I should’ve went over the reviews a little bit better as some people did say they did go up a size. For me I probably should’ve went up two sizes. In my mind I always think I’m a size 10 maybe a size 12, with these pants I probably should’ve gotten a 14. They do go super high all the way up to the small part of your waist so the part around your waist has no give no stretch. The part around your thighs your booty your legs is super stretchy, so it was hard to think what size only because I didn’t want around my legs or my booty to be super big. They are a wide legged jeans which isn’t my favorite but I honestly don’t mind these. Especially for the winter or colder months with some cute boots or maybe some UGGs. I would’ve liked for the waste part maybe to stretch a little bit more to provide a little bit more comfort. As I said they do go super high up to the smaller part of your waist almost right under your rib cage so they are super tight and there’s no give up there. I may wear these once or twice I’m kind of a little scared to wash them. I don’t want them to get smaller or tighter. I am 5‘,4“ and for how high they go they were right at my ankle so if you are taller and they do go that high up your waist they’re gonna be very short. I feel a little 50-50 on these. Like I said the gene the stretch is very nice. Nice color, but I find them too tight maybe a little too uncomfortable for a lot of wear. Maybe for a nice outing or date I can totally wear these. I’m not very happy of how high they go only because I’m not very thin and I feel like that smaller part of your stomach at the bottom Does show a lot so a longer sweater or a longer jacket will definitely be my choice.

Stylish, quality and fit. I love this jeans. It’s stylish and on trend. The fit is loose and comfortable. It’s loose from the hip down, however the waist area fits snug even though it has an elastic waistband. I ordered a size large with a 35” waist. I feel if I had ordered a size up for a more loose fit in the waist area then the rest of the jeans would be extremely loose and awkward. The quality and craftsmanship is excellent. The fabric is thick and not flimsy. The zipper and button is functional. It features pockets in the back, and deep pockets in the front, as well as a drawstring belt. The color and design is lovely. Overall this is a good quality jeans and I feel it’s worth the value.

19 weeks pregnant, perfect size So I got these and they are SO COMFY!!!! Let me just state first and foremost that I have them a 4 star review because I’m short (5ft). So they were a little long. Luckily, they don’t have sewn folds at the bottom. Otherwise, they’re cute and comfy. And for anyone reading the comments looking for what size they should order: 5ft (1.52m) 136lbs (61.69kg) I ordered a medium.

Not Disappointed - Value=Price I’m currently: 30 weeks - 5’5” - 152lbs. Have mainly gained weight in belly/boobs not so much hips/legs/butt. My impressions: opening the package, yes they have a smell. I guarantee this goes away once washed. Look: I received what is modeled regarding the color and distressing. Size: maybe a little bit long, but I like that I can wear them as a full pant or cuffed (pictured both). With how and where I carry weight a Medium felt true to size for me. Fit: I didn’t look at the size chart but what I can say is that the belly band sits a little high for me and my liking. I prefer it either completely under or over my belly. I am able to fold just the front elastic down and that sits perfectly under my belly. The legs are just a little loose - but that’s how I like my jeans to fit. Stretch: the right amount of elastic. I’m 95% positive that I’ll be able to wear these for the remainder of my pregnancy. Overall: I’m not disappointed in purchasing these jeans. I feel that the price matches the quality/value of the pants.

PT pregnant w/ 4th I am a personal trainer, 27 weeks pregnant with my 4th. All my previous maternity pants have holes and look old. I am also getting larger faster being it my 4th. So i needed comfortable, flattering, and reliable new pants. I am 5'3" and pre pregnancy i am 110-118lbs at my fittest. Now i am at 140lbs and these pants fit like a glove and feels like they will till the end. They check all the boxes for both work and home wear. From chasing my 3 little boys around, resting, to working with clients in the gym. I prefer cotton as a fabric because it is more comfy and warm to me but these feel close to the one pair of Atheleta pants gifted to me and i am definitely satisfied. Maternity pants are HARD to shop for. I hate when the crotch area bags or the belly band is uncomfortable or falls down, and so many are so thin... Not this one. I very MUCH RECOMMEND this brand to all expecting mamas. Will buy from them again.

Womens imitation leather belt We ordered this faux leather belt from QiqiLuxi and it was priced at $10 at the time. The quality of the fake leather is about average, your basic inexpensive material. The buckle and belt tip where actually heavier duty than I expected and look pretty good. The belt material, 3 stars, the simple inexpensive buckle, 5 stars. The posting itself seems misleading as this is a womens belt, it is much to thin to be considered a mens belt. The size also suggests it is good for up to 38" waists... the limit is closer it 33". The miss has 38" hips and no way this will work low low cut jeans, it was in the furtherst hole with a set of very high waisted jeans (on the miss with a 32" waist). I have 4 stars as the buckle and belt cap are nice for the price, but it could of got 3 stars as the belt itself is cheep material and shorter than expected.

Worked for me. I’m feet tall, so at the time I ordered these in size 11, I weighed 148 lbs. They felt snug compared to the size 11 I ordered in another shade. I’ve since lost more weight and the size 11 still fits fine. If your short like me, knowing that Wallflower still doesn’t make their regular jeans in petite sizes, if you roll the cuffs down on this particular style, they will fit fine like the jeans length. They came down around my ankles, if your okay with that. For some reason, I’m constantly having to pull my pants up which is annoying. I don’t need to wear a belt as these are snug. The material is medium to thick imo. Color is nice and dark. No bad smell noticed, nothing ripped yet. Oh, it also came with pet hair.
Perfect fit I am 5 feet tall weight 107. This is a size 3. Fit perfect. They are not too tight. Very comfortable. You can also roll the cuffs more.
Love em' These jeans fit perfect for me. I ordered a size large. I'm 5'6 180 pounds. They have some stretch to them as well. Good jean quality.

Very long, good style I've been looking for a decent pair of ladies cargo jeans when I came across these. They don't fit me the way it looks on the models but I'll probably still keep. For starters, they're really long. I am 5'1 and petite. After reading many reviews about these being too small, and even referencing the size chart, I bought a medium. The size small was not on prime. The pants are definitely a size too big on my waist. I like the loose fit, but I may need to use a belt. My waist is a 27 inch, and I usually wear a size 4. I washed and dried hoping they'd shrink a bit more but they did not. Tucking in a shirt helps make them fit better as well. Order your normal size. When the small comes back on prime I'll definitely try them.

As cute as they are in the picture I’ve had my eye on these jeans for a while. I hesitate buying jeans without trying them on though, because it’s a hit or miss if they fit or not. I’m so glad I ordered them. I love these jeans!! The quality is great, they don’t feel like cheap or generic denim. They have good stretch too, and comfortable enough to move around in. The inseam is pretty long though. Even with 2-3 inch heels I still have to fold them at the bottom. Then again, I am only 5’2” lol. I like how it has the drawstring at the bottom, bc I was able to pull them a little tighter to prevent from dragging too much when I’m wearing my platform Chucks. In the picture I’m wearing chunky 3 in heels and with it rolled up once, they’re the perfect length. I followed the size chart and bought the right size (XL) according to the size chart. They fit perfect. For reference, I have a 33.5 in waist, curvy hips, I have some booty back there lol and thick thighs. And they fit like a glove on me. I felt like they hugged me in all the right spots. If you want the loose, baggy look, I would size up. But I love these jeans so much and I feel like they can be dressed up or dressed down. They remind me of the jeans we wore in high school, and I love the flap pockets on the butt. It’s more flattering for your booty. Only reason I gave 4 stars is because they are super long. And with the drawstring, can’t really hem them. It would be awesome if made these with different inseam lengths. Still love the jeans either way, these jeans are gonna be a part of my regular rotation.

10/10 I'm a person that HATES tight jeans because the material is so uncomfortable and itchy that when I wear MOST skinny jeans I can't wait to go home and take them off. I was expecting these jeans to be the same since they were from amazon but when I put them on I was so suprised that the material was soft quality and didn't make me dread wearing them. I wore them out and my BF when he saw the jeans on me had his eyes practically pop out of his head. These jeans hug every inch of my curves and they are so comfortable to wear for long hours. I could sleep in them and coming from ME that says alot. I will be purchasing more of these colors and others from here. I don't know how these will old up and if the quality of the fabric will remain the same after wash so I plan to wash on cold and hang dry these jeans to avoide any issues. I love these jeans so much. On my way to buy more pairs. BTW: The top I am wearing is a black turtle neck bodysuit jumpsuit (long sleeves) that I also purchased here on amazon. Both pieces plus a pair of black flats really put me together like I said when I took my jacket off my boyfriend's eyes popped out of his sockets because the jeans and the turtle neck bodysuit hug my whole body. MEASUREMENTS: I'm 5'3 148 pounds and I bought a size 11 and it fits perfectly. A size 13 would have been to large and not as flattering. size 11 is not to big still plenty of stretch to material to not hug your body tight. I normally wear a sieze 10 in jeans so I went up one size. Best decision ever. GET THESE JEANS!!!

Comfortable! I got a size 7, but I probably could’ve gone another size up since it’s tight around the waist. I do have a scar on my stomach, but the button doesn’t pinch it so it’s not painful (I don’t know my hip size, but I’m around 120 lbs), but for someone else who has bigger hips or stomach, I would recommend going 2 sizes up. Keep in mind the waistline will be the tightest part compared to the rest of the jeans. Overall, the pants are actually fairly comfortable and the material isn’t scratchy at all. It’s somewhat stretchy. Some jeans can be irritating if you don’t shave your legs, but this pair is comfy regardless if you shave or not! It’s only been one day, and the zipper keeps riding down when moving around. I don’t know if it’s because the waistline of the jeans is too small for me or if it’s just a general issue, but it isn’t a big deal. Jeans are a big long too, since I’m 5’3 and it kind of scrunches up around the ankles, but that’s an easy fix! I don’t have hardly any curves and my butt is flat as can be, but the pants did give me some of a figure (not by much haha!). It also didn’t slide down while bending, so that’s a plus. I never wore high waist jeans before, but this might turn me over.

Magical Mom Jeans make me look more Shapely & Curvaceous Stretchy, Soft, Well Made I must say, I am very pleased I bought these magical mom jeans in black and medium wash. My waist is 25.5", 33.5" hips. I ordered size 3. They fit beautifully. I have smallish hips and a wider waist so I was worried that the waist band would be too tight. It is snug, but it does have some stretch, so I think as long as I don't gain weight around my waist, they should be comfy. I have only tried these on so far, I haven't actually worn them anywhere or washed them. They are soft and stretchy. I thought the black might be more crispy and less stretchy, but they are about the same. The fit on both pair was identical. The jeans really make the most of my small butt, and they make my waist look smaller, which gives the illusion of more of a heart-shape, when in reality, my waist to hip transition is pretty pretty straight. I can do a deep squat in these jeans without having to adjust them. In the pictures I am wearing them with panties that create lines. I don't want these to shrink, so I will probably wash in cold and hang dry. The fabric looks like it has good structure and is unlikely to stretch out or sag, but time will tell. I paid less than $30 each, which I think is a great price for what I received. Plus I ordered them day before yesterday and they arrived this morning. Can't beat that!

Highly recommend! Super retro I have been looking for high waisted jeans that were high enough to cover my belly button, I have some cute crop tops that I wanted to wear without exposing my stomach. I searched the net and these were the only review pics sent in that showed the jeans covering the belly button. They are more like jeggings than jeans which I love because they have great stretch, soft, and comfy, yet they look like jeans. You get the best of both worlds. I'm 5'2 120lbs I got the size 3. I could tell they were long from other reviews so I planned to roll them up anyway giving them more of Vintage look which was what I was going for. Originally I was going to go up a size to a 5 but after reading the reviews I figured a 3 would work best and it did. They looked too small when I opened the bag but They had great stretch and fit me just the way I wanted them to. I highly recommend these and am ordering more. I'm not sure how long they will last as I just wore once but honestly I will buy again even if they last only through out the year. I'd gladly buy a pair year, as long as they didn't get crazy expensive. I wouldn't normally spend $30 on jeans from online but since I couldn't find any super high waisted jeans Anywhere else I thought to give these a try

May not need to size up. I am 5’9 - 137 - 25.5-26 inch waist and 36.5 hip and the size 5 were a little too loose. I’m so sad bc they are so cute so I’m exchanging them for 3. I like a tight fit and there was so much room in the hips and stomach area for the size 5. Should have gone with 3’s. They are tight in the calves but mine are like jokingly tiny so I could see how that may be a problem for someone else. Update: (picture included) I like the 3’s better. I probably could have gone with 1’s for reference. May pick up 1s later but the 3s are nice. I’m pretty happy with the butt but it could be slightly more flattering with the back pockets in my opinion but great for me to wear these as they’re extremely comfy and I’m a leggings or skirt/dress person. They are a little loose in the crotch so keep that in mind you may be able to go smaller if we have a similar figure to me and you like a tight fit.

I’m 5 months postpartum so I haven’t been quite sure about what size I am. I love that this product has free returns because the original size I ordered was too tight (i originally ordered size 9 but returned them for size 11). Im currently 157 pounds with most of my weight being in my thighs and butt. Size 11 fits just right (pics attached). Size 13 may fit even better, but my goal is to loose some more weight so Im keeping the 11. The pants feel comfty (not itchy or stiff). I can bend in them now that theyve fitted to my body. The button is just on my belly button. The pants have two long back pockets (fits my iphone). I want to get the other colors. I would def recommend.

I honestly feel like these will become my go-to outfit this fall and I can't wait to wear them again and again!

I was reading... too small, annoying stripes, etc. That's because these are small. They are super stretchy, but have a snug waistband. They are surprisingly slimming... if you order right. I am 5'5" and 150 and a 3-c-section belly. I usually wear size medium or size 8-10. This is why I ordered the 1\2x and they fit absolutely comfy and amazing. These have no negatives on any part of my body for ordering big and no overly stretchy lines. I just think their size chart and measurements are like Japanese and European that run extremely small and make us feel fat. Lol. I hope this helps. I will absolutely order more colors and in the bigger size.

Super comfy and accurate sizing! I was looking for a comfy skort I could wear in the summer and this delivered! I was prepared to be underwhelmed because it had no reviews when I ordered it lol. It definitely exceeded expectations! I'm 5'5, 122lbs and usually around 25in for bottoms. The sizing chart was very accurate and the small fits well! I probably could have gone with an XS for a more snug fit, but I wanted a little room for a more relaxed fit. This is a very comfortable, slightly oversized feel, like a boyfriend jean. The pockets are very deep and the material is soft. Went in the washer and dryer with no problems. Would highly recommend!

Replacing my discontinued jeans for these. I want to be as detailed as possible. Firstly as I write this it's more of first impressions than an actual review. I'm 5'9" and currently weigh 243lbs. I ordered these pants based off of Amazon's recommendations in size 18. These pants just fit. And I don't mean that in a bad way. They are not too big or too tight anywhere. They just fit me. Lol. I got some junk in the trunk. I struggle with chub rub and wide calves. Again nothing feels constrained. The material has that rough texture like normal jeans. It's made with 3% spandex, which surprisingly gives a lot of stretch overall to the pants. My usual go to pair of high waisted black jeans have been Old Navy's Rockstar super skinny high rise 24/7 sculpt pants in size 14 regular. These pants are a pretty close match. Those jeans have been my everything. I work all day in those pants and live in those pants. And I can't get them anymore. That's how I ended up here trying these pants. I could probably size down to a 16 for a more fitted pair. But I'm not going to return these ones. They are high rise for sure. If you need pants to work in like me these will do. I can bend/squat down in these and not fear I'm revealing my backside. I did see a review before ordering these about the back pockets being more outward/on the side. And they might be further outward than most jeans. But that's okay with me. I carry my phone in my back pocket so having the pockets more outward prevents me from crushing my phone. Overall, I'm happy with the purchase and the first impressions. I don't know how well they will hold up with lots of use. I go through jeans and shoes frequently. I do a lot walking/bending/squating etc at work. And maintain an active lifestyle outside of work too. So these pants will get a lot of work out of them. If anything major happens I will update this review. Hope this helps.

For men too! I love these pants! I have never found a pair of men's jeans that fit right. Most mainstream men's skinny jeans are too baggy or too stiff. And most non mainstream men's skinny jeans are overpriced and too cramped where it matters. But these are perfect! I love the length, the high waist (for extra room for your jonson), the stretchy-ness, the price, and the style! For reference I am 5'8", 130lbs, 28" waist, and I fit a size 5 perfectly. At first I bought the zipper fly pair and have worn them for a while. I wanted a second pair since they ended up becoming my favorite pair of jeans, so I decided to buy another. Except they only had the button fly jeans available. I am now a button fly guy. I like them a lot. They look better than zipper fly IMO. If there was one thing I would critique it would be the ability for the jeans to hold shape after a few days. Day one they fit perfectly, day two a little more lose but not bad, day three and you might need a belt, day four and they are for sure going to sag without a belt. Wash them up and they fit perfectly again. It's not that bad since four days and the pants should probably be washed anyway. I guess the last critique is that they attract a LOT of fur. If you have cats or dogs, beware! Buy some lint rollers.

Comfortable and versatile denim shorts Finally, I found a pair of denim shorts that I can comfortable wear year round! Generally, I avoid denims because they are difficult to fit properly and are too hot to wear in the warm climates I always live in. But, I always thought denims look good and go with just about every top. This pair of Awuka denim shorts solved my problem. The material is very stretchy and doesn't bind anywhere. The cut is modest being high waisted and with the hem length. The pockets are deep enough. The stitching is good. I did trim off the fray strings at the hem as that's not me. Importantly, the material is not hot. I machine washed and tumbled dry and it didn't shrink. The small fits me quite well. My waist is 28 inches, weight is 120 lbs and height is 5'5". I would purchase this again.

Soft & Comfy, Pricey, But Not A Great Fit For Pear-Shaped Women, I *WILL* Luncle Dry! I'm just under 5'8" and down to 195lbs at the time of my order. I lost over 60 lbs last year, from a size 22-24 to I'm not sure what size exactly. This might be important for others to know. I'm still losing slowly, but based on the measurements I took of myself before ordering (Waist 39", Hips 45", Inseam 32-34"), I optimistically ordered size 14 -- and miraculously, they fit, just a bit snug but not overly tight. I think the slight stretch to the material helped with that. The tag was so amusing that I had to share. I'm too old to deal with hand washing anything, so if it doesn't survive my machine, it's not worth having. You can bet that not only did I wash it in my machine (cold water, normal setting, with the rest of my clothes), I also tumbled it dry -- to be fair, I did follow the instructions to not luncle dry it! They came out just fine, with no noticeable shrinkage. If machine washing & drying makes them wear out faster, that's just too bad. The most I'll go out of my way for something special I rarely wear is to put it in a net bag to wash and hang to dry -- but that's only if I have the time and inclination. :) The only problem I have with them is the same as with many pants: there is a gap at my lower back since my hips & butt are bigger than my waist. I have to wear a belt with these pants to correct that. They are not really made for women with my type of curves. I'm also a notorious penny-pincher, and these pants are nearly $40, which is almost twice as much as I prefer to pay for pants. These are comfy & soft, but the material is also thin, so I don't know how long they'll last before they tear. I've only worn them a few times since I received & washed them. For this price, I hope they last a long time. If they do tear or have any problems prematurely, I will come back to update.

BEST PANT EVER. Super stretchy! Stretches out over time - good to size down I bought these first in white in a 6, and LOVED THEM, then they became baggy as I wore them (in a cute way though). So I bought the grey and red in a 4 and they fit perfect. I normally go between a 4-6 in normal pants so I'd recommend sizing down because of the stretch. These are my new favorite pants. I have worn them the last 4 times I have gone out. Perfect for any occasion, and great with a bodysuit. I have a small waist and wider hips and the 4's fit perfectly, no belt needed.

This will be the most HONEST REVIEW you've ever seen..... I bought the Black in size 12 and the Green in size 14, but only bcuz a Green size 12 didn't become available Until AFTER I let the seller know they were running out of sizes. However, I have tried Shrinking the Green size 14 and they did shrink a little bit. I will try to Shrink the Green some more, but I will need to Stretch the Legs while they are hot and/or Use a Steamer to Stretch the Length, bcuz I really don't want the Length to Shrink! Also, as you will see in the photos, the Black pair had Tags etc strung into a little black loop inside the back of the pants, whereas the Green pair didn't have any Tags nor a special little black loop for the Tags, so I almost kinda feel that the Green pair were a Return, but that's ok, bcuz I've since washed them... But truthfully, I'm shocked that these pants/jeans are as Amazing, Soft and Super Comfy as they are! When I first grabbed the package from being downstairs and outside at the door, it didn't feel like a package of jeans. It felt waaaaay too light for jeans and I was almost disappointed! But... I opened the package and decided to try them on anyway! Wow!! I am absolutely blown away! Not only do they Fit according to the Size Chart, (I'm 5' - 6.5" and about 145ish pounds) but they are a really nice, soft and a "comfy around the waist" pair of jeans, that I honestly think will become my Favorite pair of jeans!! Due to stomach issues, I don't usually like anything around or touching my upper tummy, but these are sooo Light and Soft that they honestly Don't bother my tummy 1 bit! I'm even been sitting and laying on the couch to check out their comfortness and they pass with flying colors.. COMFORT Beyond Belief!! And bcuz of how extremely Comfortable they are, I am now going to order a couple more pairs!I've already ordered and received the Green pair, but I'm going to get another Black pair like these ones, and I think I'll get a pair of the Darker Blue ones IF the seller has more of the Blue ones in a size 12?!? I do have 1 "Slight" issue, and it's nothing major, it's a "Me Thing" just bcuz I'm very picky when it comes to a Pants Length, I do prefer my pants to be Longer, and so, I wish these jeans were another Inch, or even preferably 2 inches Longer!! But as they are right now, they almost touch the floor at my 5' - 6.5" of height! And like I said, it's a "Me Thing", I just prefer Extra Long Pants!! However, after washing them and before hanging them up to dry, I'm going to try and Stretch the Legs out in order to Gain a little bit more Length!! And IF this doesn't work, I may have to use Hot Steam to Stretch them out a bit, bcuz Hot Steam always works when wanting to Stretch out clothing!! **I have since washed both pairs in cool water and tumbled dried them for a bit with a little bit of Heat and I was able to stretch the Length back.** Anyway, being an extremely Picky person, I personally and highly Recommend these jeans! You Won't be Disappointed!! I truly hope you Grow to Love them as much as I do, and I've only had them on for 30 minutes just to try them out... (I've now had them for 2 days and have washed both the Black and Green pair and All was good) But I do know already, that they will and have, become my FAVORITE pair of Cargo jeans... And yes, it's true that they are Not a Heavy Cotton jean, but that's why I Love them so much, bcuz with them being a lighter fabric, it makes them Easier to move around in! I can do Squats and whatever else in them, plus Sit for Hours in them and I'm still very comfortable and it's all bcuz of how Flexible they are with being a Lighter fabric/material... --Bottom Line, I have absolutely NO PROBLEM with these Cargo jeans! They are absolutely Amazing and I'm sure you will Love them as much as I do...--

This will be the most HONEST REVIEW you've ever seen..... I bought the Black in size 12 and the Green in size 14, but only bcuz a Green size 12 didn't become available Until AFTER I let the seller know they were running out of sizes. However, I have tried Shrinking the Green size 14 and they did shrink a little bit. I will try to Shrink the Green some more, but I will need to Stretch the Legs while they are hot and/or Use a Steamer to Stretch the Length, bcuz I really don't want the Length to Shrink! Also, as you will see in the photos, the Black pair had Tags etc strung into a little black loop inside the back of the pants, whereas the Green pair didn't have any Tags nor a special little black loop for the Tags, so I almost kinda feel that the Green pair were a Return, but that's ok, bcuz I've since washed them... But truthfully, I'm shocked that these pants/jeans are as Amazing, Soft and Super Comfy as they are! When I first grabbed the package from being downstairs and outside at the door, it didn't feel like a package of jeans. It felt waaaaay too light for jeans and I was almost disappointed! But... I opened the package and decided to try them on anyway! Wow!! I am absolutely blown away! Not only do they Fit according to the Size Chart, (I'm 5' - 6.5" and about 145ish pounds) but they are a really nice, soft and a "comfy around the waist" pair of jeans, that I honestly think will become my Favorite pair of jeans!! Due to stomach issues, I don't usually like anything around or touching my upper tummy, but these are sooo Light and Soft that they honestly Don't bother my tummy 1 bit! I'm even been sitting and laying on the couch to check out their comfortness and they pass with flying colors.. COMFORT Beyond Belief!! And bcuz of how extremely Comfortable they are, I am now going to order a couple more pairs!I've already ordered and received the Green pair, but I'm going to get another Black pair like these ones, and I think I'll get a pair of the Darker Blue ones IF the seller has more of the Blue ones in a size 12?!? I do have 1 "Slight" issue, and it's nothing major, it's a "Me Thing" just bcuz I'm very picky when it comes to a Pants Length, I do prefer my pants to be Longer, and so, I wish these jeans were another Inch, or even preferably 2 inches Longer!! But as they are right now, they almost touch the floor at my 5' - 6.5" of height! And like I said, it's a "Me Thing", I just prefer Extra Long Pants!! However, after washing them and before hanging them up to dry, I'm going to try and Stretch the Legs out in order to Gain a little bit more Length!! And IF this doesn't work, I may have to use Hot Steam to Stretch them out a bit, bcuz Hot Steam always works when wanting to Stretch out clothing!! **I have since washed both pairs in cool water and tumbled dried them for a bit with a little bit of Heat and I was able to stretch the Length back.** Anyway, being an extremely Picky person, I personally and highly Recommend these jeans! You Won't be Disappointed!! I truly hope you Grow to Love them as much as I do, and I've only had them on for 30 minutes just to try them out... (I've now had them for 2 days and have washed both the Black and Green pair and All was good) But I do know already, that they will and have, become my FAVORITE pair of Cargo jeans... And yes, it's true that they are Not a Heavy Cotton jean, but that's why I Love them so much, bcuz with them being a lighter fabric, it makes them Easier to move around in! I can do Squats and whatever else in them, plus Sit for Hours in them and I'm still very comfortable and it's all bcuz of how Flexible they are with being a Lighter fabric/material... --Bottom Line, I have absolutely NO PROBLEM with these Cargo jeans! They are absolutely Amazing and I'm sure you will Love them as much as I do...--

Sleeves fit perfectly I love this white GV petite jean jacket. It fits perfectly. I'm 4'11 at 119 lbs and ordered a medium which fits very well. The sleeves are a good length and not too long. I'm so happy I don't have to roll up the sleeves. I wear jackets and sweaters year around because I get cold in stores and restraunts especially in the warm months so I'll be using it a lot. Non see through white pant Very nice heavy wide leg white pant. I would say they are a little on the extra wide side through the leg and that would be ok. The only problem with these pants is that they run really long. I am 5’7” and without a heal the cover my feet and then some. With a heal they are still long, but definitely do able. I would recommend these pants for someone over 5’8”.

Too long for my 5'6" height Despite being a size 12, I ordered these pants with wide legs in a medium. With a 34-36" waist and ample room in the thigh area, I assumed the pants would be a good fit and they were. However at 5'6", the pants are excessively long, and I would advise those shorter to be warned. In the advertisement, the material of the slacks appears to be of higher quality than it actually is, but given the summer heat, I was not bothered by the difference. The front pleat did nothing to flatter my stomach (pudge). But, the pants' deep pockets did contribute to its comfort. In general, I'm not sure if I can wear these without alterations or without the hemline touching the ground, even with heels.

It features a zipper, and that area is recessed so you can set things on top. The two pockets in front are functional!! The strap I adjusted to its longest length, and it it’s my 5’4 frame at the hip when worn crossbody. When not taking this to work, I have tons more room because I don’t need the speaker or markers. I love it!!

Perfect little denim jacket Perfection! I bought this in both White and Wishful Blue. They are both a soft, comfy denim fabric with a slightly fitted shape. Not too fitted, but not that typical stiff boxy jeans jacket. At 5’2” somewhere between a 12 -14, I was pleased to find the size Large was the perfect fit. The “hem” hits me right above/at the hips, which is perfect for wearing over a spring/summer dress. I love the pockets, which are big enough to actually tuck your hands into. I like that it’s not too distressed. No rips or holes; just the frayed hem and a few “worn” spots. The wishful blue color is so pretty, It’s a nice worn blue. Not white washed. Not acid washed. And definitely not that weird yellowish dirty denim that I hate. Another reviewer called it cropped, which is not my opinion at all. It doesn’t cover my tush, but it hits lower than my waist. Delivery was quick. It arrived in one day. Hope this helps you find your best size and color.

I was curious how this cut of jean would fit me but wanted to give it a try. I actually loved it. The jeans are well made and run pretty close to size. For reference I am 5' 2" and 115lbs and usually wear a size 4. These run from small to x-large. I ordered the small and there was still a little room but it is supposed to be a relaxed fit. They are a good weight but not a heavy jean. They are a cropped fit and being a shorty it still ended up being slightly cropped so if you are a taller girlie it will crop even higher on your leg. They are very comfortable and have a slight stretch to them as well. All in all I am very happy with these pants.

My New Favorite Winter Jeans Love these jeans! I've had fleece lined jeans before from LL Bean and I'd say these are definitely a contender for the savings. These came out of the packaging a little smelly, even after their first wash smelled a little yuck. But they aired out and the smell is totally gone. They have good stretch and are VERY cozy and soft on the inside so they really feel like fuzzy yoga leggings on. I'm happy with the length. I'm about 5'6" and these hit just at the ankle, which is perfect because they won't drag the ground and get wet when it's damp outside. I like how they look, great color, good rise length, and not thick so they stay kinda flattering. Overall, I definitely recommend them if you're looking for an affordable and comfortable pair of warm jeans for cool weather.

These are nice and I may buy more now I ride a motorcycle and sometimes it is a bit chilly on the legs like in the evenings or early mornings in spring and fall and these help with that. They'd also be great for sporting events and festivals when the weather isn't the warmest. These are nicer than adding leggings under your pants because you don't have to mess with 2 layers as these are all in one. They're very soft and feel nice on the skin and the jean material is nice and heavy and not thin. They're like a straight leg cut and fit nice around the waste. I am between a 10 & 12 size depending upon how they're made and I'm 5'6.5"; I got a large and they're a perfect fit.

A "skinny" jean actually cut to fit a curvy body! Given that I am curvy (a pretty traditional pear shape with a smaller waist and larger hips, bottom, and thighs), I have always been wary of "skinny" jeans and have tended to stick with more boot-cut styles. After the rave reviews about these jeans for curvy women, I decided to try them. I am most often a size 8 in jeans, but I got these in a 10, as I thought that I would need the extra size due to the "skinny" cut.These jeans are a dark rinse with a slight whiskering. They are described as having a "flex and flatter fabric," and I would say that this is true--there is a nice stretch to them, and they comfortable hugged my curves. The length was perfect for me (5'4.5"), just past the ankles. I do think a size 10 was the correct size for me in this style, although as you can see from my photos, I did still have a slight gap to the waistband at the back. However, since these jeans have a hidden elasticity to the waist, the waistband did hug my body, and the small gap was barely noticeable. The waistband hit just above my hip bones and right at my belly button. These jeans were also flattering to the booty, with a nice placement to the pockets plus a curved yoke/shaping darts.I have machined washed/dried these jeans, and they seemed to turn out well with no shrinkage. Overall, these are definitely a nice pair of jeans for the curvy woman, and I would recommend me them, especially for those who have a bit less of a waist-to-hip ratio than I do.1/11/23 UPDATE:I still have these and they have become my favorite jeans! I've gained a little weight with menopause, so either that or the jeans shrinking slightly have helped them to fit better. They are comfortably stretchy and feel just right.

Great fit! I was hesitant to purchase jeans online because I wasn't sure what I would do if they didn't fit. It's one thing to purchase another pair of jeans when you already have a pair and you know your size. It's completely different to purchase a pair blind without having a reference point. So, I looked at the reviews and I saw where a woman who is 5'3" says they fit her well. She said they fit "perfect". I'm 5'2' so I was SOLD because of her review. I mean, the jeans look great on the model, but I wasn't sure they'd look or feel great on me, especially since I'm short. Well, blow me over with a feather! i am so excited I pulled the trigger. The color is beautiful. They're a deep, dark blue. The length is perfect. Like I said, I'm an inch shorter than the other woman and that 1 inch does not make them go from the perfect length to too long. They fit well in the stomach. I have a stomach. I birthed two children and I'm fat. Unless I pay a doctor in Mexico to fix this, that stomach and I will be best friends until I die. They are not too tight. They stretch to accommodate my belly well. I usually wear a 12 which fit my belly well, but are too big in the legs. They sag, and I'm not a 20 year old boy who sags on purpose. i just don't have enough going on to fill out the pants. i chose a 10. It was available and I'm losing weight by eating keto. Although I've lost 6 or 7 pounds and these fit GREAT, they would fit better if I lost another 5 pounds. I don't have a great shaped lower (or upper) body and they're not magic, but they look good and feel good. I don't know about sewing, but whatever "darts" they added to enhance my shape did a pretty decent job.

Truly Sculpting! The most comfortable jeans ! I do not like trying on pants or jeans I avoid it at all costs they are very tricky to fit on my curvy very short 61 year old body. However these were a winner ! I purchased the F'AB BODY SCULPT SKINNY style. These jeans have lots of stretch, the waistband in particular is so comfortable and it is high enough to cover muffin tops. They are intended to be ankle length but I have to hem up about 2" as I am 5'2". The sculpting darts and elastic panels really do work ! I am size 8 but originally purchased size 10 based on some reviews and I was nervous that an 8 would be too tight. The 10 fit but after wearing a few days the waistband loosened up and I was constantly hiking them up. I switched that out for a size 8 . I recommend trying your usual size in the SCULPT SKINNY style.

Runs a bit small but I love it!! I LOVE this belt! I already purchased the other two colors, however I did size up in those as I find this runs a bit small. For reference, I am 5'7" and 135lbs with a 27-28" waist. I ordered the small and wearing them with high waisted jeans, it fits fine. There is enough slack where it doesn't look funny. But if I were to wear these with anything lower waisted, there would not be enough slack and it might look funny. I will update once I get the medium in the other colors how they fit. I have a belt hole punch if I need to add more holes. I do find it a bit tricky to get the belt buckled and unbuckled but its literally because of the shape of the buckle, not a flaw in the product. Once you get it buckled and laid flat, it looks great but getting it on at first, I was a bit worried! I noticed another reviewer said the gold was not a good color, but I like it. It doesn't look cheap to me. Overall, I really love this belt. Its a much more fun alternative to standard belts. It can easily be worn with jeans or with dress pants for the office. I would consider sizing up but if you are on the high end of the size range provided. Definitely recommend!!!
