Serviceable but Don't Love It I feel that this runs a little large in comparison to the size chart. I just ordered my usual size instead, M, and overall the fit is ok and matches up to a typical M, I am just a bit too short & chubby for these to fit right. I am 5'3", 38-33-38 and the hem is about 3 inches too long, rise is too long for my taste (definitely a high waist on me when I would have preferred mid-rise). This is a very fine-boned corduroy fabric (see pic) and it does not stretch a lot apart from the elastic waist, so it's more like real pants than stretchy leggings. You may possibly want to order up and think of it more like baggy comfy pants? Or it may fit better on someone with a trim body. These won't fit well if you have large thighs (mine are 20" and I feel that they're about the max) or bootie, I think they will drag down on you. Overall these will probably work the best on someone with a 30-32" inseam & a slim-rectangle kind of body type. Looking at other reviewers that seems to match up with who was more happy with these pants. Really nice color selection (I was so tempted by both the greens but am surprisingly lacking in plain black pants) & they look good but they're a bit ill-fitting for me on comfort.

This is a very fine-boned corduroy fabric (see pic) and it does not stretch a lot apart from the elastic waist, so it's more like real pants than stretchy leggings. You may possibly want to order up and think of it more like baggy comfy pants? Or it may fit better on someone with a trim body. These won't fit well if you have large thighs (mine are 20" and I feel that they're about the max) or bootie, I think they will drag down on you. Overall these will probably work the best on someone with a 30-32" inseam & a slim-rectangle kind of body type. Looking at other reviewers that seems to match up with who was more happy with these pants. Really nice color selection (I was so tempted by both the greens but am surprisingly lacking in plain black pants) & they look good but they're a bit ill-fitting for me on comfort.

Fun and comfortable These are comfortable pants with a good amount of stretch to them. My wife wasn’t sure what to expect with these pants but she is happy with them. The waist is elastic, but there are no belt loops and no front pockets. For reference she is 5’4 and about 120lbs and likes how the small fits her. They are a high waist fit and the length is just right, just barely touched the floor without shoes on. Overall she likes them. The color is a little more golden brown than khaki in person, but she still likes it and thinks they are versatile. The fit is flattering and comfortable. The quality could be better but is okay for the price but she thinks the front pockets and belt loops would add a lot to them. I hope this assists you with your shopping. Waist is a Little Too High and Length Could Be Longer for Me, But Still Very Wearable and Comfy I got a size 6 and the fit is pretty good. I typically go back and forth between size 4 and 6 on jeans depending on brand. Since I never owned this brand before, I took a shot at a 6. The fit is fine except the crotch depth and waist height is a little too deep for my figure, but that is typical on me with other brands where I get a size 6. All that said, I would guess the jeans are close to true to size.I am 5’8” and the jeans are long enough to wear, but only with flatter shoes. They fall to the back of my heal. I prefer a jean that almost touches the floor in the back, so these are about an inch to 1-1/2” shorter than I like. Once again, I find this problem with other brands so I don’t think this is a sizing issue, just a figure issue for me. (I’m kind of short waisted and long legged).The jeans are made well and I like the fabric. They feel like “broken in” jeans from the moment you put them on (a big plus for me). They feel nice on the skin. The fabric is also a nice weight - not too thin or too thick. I found them comfortable to wear. The waist stayed in place and they didn’t “bag” in any areas after a day of wearing. The color is perfect. I do like the straight leg cut of the jean. To me it’s much more comfortable than skinny jeans.I have never owned jeans with cargo pockets before, so this is something new. I have mixed feelings on the pockets. I think it’s just something I need to find the correct top to go with them and they will be fine.Overall, I think these are a great jean and run true to size. Five stars.

My search for the perfect cargo jeans is over! I have been struggling to find the perfect pair of cargo jeans until now...these ETELLO jeans are everything! The quality of the jeans are great! Darling jeans, surprised at the price These are really flattering and comfortable - if it weren't for the lower quality button closure used in the front waistband and the need to wear these a bit to get the painted on lines to look natural, I would think these would be a lot more expensive. I wear a 28 in Mother Denim / Anthropologie and a size small fits great. I think if you are under a size 6 you would find the small too baggy. Keep in mind there is a LOT of stretch in these so you'll be comfy and should feel better about buying in S/M/L (I've always bought jeans in exact sizes so was surprised it would come in just those categories). The wide leg is very flattering and the large pockets in the back look great with a top. Will try to remember to snap a picture and upload. I told a friend they were from amazon and under $50 and she said, "no way." For reference I'm 5'7" 145lbs, wearing size small in "Lapis." The hem fits perfectly with flats for my height, might be a little long for the shorter girls but cuffed jeans are back in style.

Green One Piece Plus Size Jump Suit The Green All-in-One Plus Size arrived sooner than expected with Amazon Prime.This Plus Size one piece runs small in the tummy, hips and thigh areas. It is designed for someone with a long turso and height over 5'4". The sleeves and legs of the one piece are long, if you have short arms and legs, it gathers well.It is not designed for anyone owning a foopah. It is not designed for that shape.The one piece is beautiful, the color is as pictured and it is not sheer, so there is no see through the material. However, there is no give in the outfit and to be comfortable for sitting and removing if you have to go to the bathroom requires you to size up 1 size for comfort and flexibility. I purchased a 2x, I am between 221 and 241. I am about 5'2". It does not flatter my pear shape, petite body type, however, I will try to make it work because it is very pretty.Wore it Today at Church for Palm Sunday. It was a HIT. Folks actually touched it to feel the material, it is gorgeous. I will be passing this jumpsuit on to someone who has the height and shape for it. Really nice jumpsuit, runs slim in the torso, stomach and hips area. However, it is rather flattering on the body. I did not wear the belt, I could not find it after having this in the closet for so long. The belt was not missed.

Very long crotch area but overall good! I really like this jumpsuit. I am 5’8, 170 lbs. The material is flowy with no stretch. Thin, but not see through. I like that the chest area has a small button for extra coverage if needed. I am 36DDD and the chest is not tight, which was a relief. My reason for 4 stars instead of 5 was the extra long crotch (rise). I love high rise pants, but if you have a larger hip/ booty area, this will cause tightness and the pockets to gape as you can see on some of the other review photos. The crotch hangs well below where it should. My hips are 44’ if that helps. Overall, great buy for the price and the green is a very vibrant Kelly green.

Not realistic model photos 😔 These are a dupe of EB Denim’s Fredrick Jean. I was super excited to find these since the original retails for 400$. However, the photos on Amazon are of the original, not the dupe. These are a light weight denim in a light wash with faded spots on the front and back of thighs that are almost white (and not flattering). The 3 seams across the knees are so cute. There is a fit issue for me - too much fabric in the front crotch area. It bunches up weird. I’m 5’4 and these just skim the ground, perfect for wearing flats or low heels. I’m usually a 14/16 in pants and the XL fits. However, I’m used to stretch denim and these are non-stretch. They do have an elastic back to the waist band. I find them a little uncomfortable in the front waist area. They are more straight legged than barrel jean. I think these are a return for me.

I like the off-center buttons and zipper but wish it was more exaggerated. The strip of fabric for the topmost button is a bit thin. When there's a lot of movement or while pulling up the waist, my tummy peeks through. I'm now a true size 6 and love how they look. The fit is more baggy and slouchy and has a relaxed off-beat vibe. I would suggest ordering 1 size up.

Perfect jeans! I absolutely love these jeans! For reference I’m 5’2”, 115 lbs, 25” waist, 34 1/2” hips. I sized up and got a 26 and it fits perfectly. ACTUAL low rise jeans!! Ok, first of all, these are authentic. All pockets are real pockets, and they are extremely well made. I'm 5'0", 108 lbs, and I bought the 27s. The fit is PERFECT, but I will definitely need to have them hemmed. For now I've been folding the excess pant leg up and under, and it works just fine, because the fit is slim enough that it stays in place all day without budging.I can't gush enough about how thrilled I am to have found these jeans. The high rise pant trend needs to die. It's not really flattering on anyone, but it is murder on us short girls. At only 5'0", even "low rise" jeans come up so high on me that the waist band always covers my belly button. Some "low rise" jeans come up so high on my torso I can practically tuck my tits into them. Not only is it crazy unflattering, but it's incredibly uncomfortable. The jeans always dug into my stomach when I sat down. For years I have had to rearrange the waistband of my jeans every time I sat. I so missed the 90's style, "sit so low on your hips you flash your crack every time you sit" jeans. They were so much more comfortable and flattering on me! I've been searching for years, but couldn't find anyone that still makes a true "sits on the hips" jean. And then I found these! I couldn't believe it when I put them on. After trying on hundreds of pairs of "low rise" jeans that came up to above my belly button, these actually sit right at my hip bone, right where they should. And unlike the 90's low rise jeans, these fit so well they don't have that weird gap in the back of the waistband when you sit, and they don't flash your undies. They stay put. They are AMAZING. So flattering and comfortable.The only slight drawback is that the back pockets do sit a pretty low, so they're not the most flattering butt jeans ever. And if you're someone who is constantly shoving your phone in your back pocket like I am, the pocket flap is not the most convenient thing ever. But those are minor issues for me, considering how overall thrilled I am with these. After the first pair arrived and I saw how well they fit I immediately ordered two more pairs, and they are equally amazing. Love 'em.

Love.. love them!! It's been years since I've bought a good pair of jeans, and I hate wearing high waist jeans, these are amazing!! They fit sooooo nice, stretchy and comfortable. I'm 5'7 and weigh about 150

Just the right amount of baggy comfort I ordered these in the medium (unisex) size––I’m 5’5”, 140lbs and I found the medium to be the perfect amount of baggy without swimming in them so much that I feel like I have no shape. The elastic on both the ankle and waist is nice and not at all tight; the drawstring is just a simple cotton shoe-lace style rope; and there are no grommets to keep the string from disappearing into the elastic waistband. For me, the backside is a little saggy, but that’ll be different for everyone. Plus it will shrink over time because it’s cotton. The fabric is medium to lightweight, it’s slightly fleeced on the inside and a mostly soft outside with some texture to it, which is similar to a fine, corduroy stripe. Pepper is a great, dusty dark grey that goes with everything. I’m happy enough with them that I’m thinking of also buying them in blue.

I sized up because these are 100% cotton. Very comfortable, relaxed fit I recently got a pair of these grey (“pepper”) 100% cotton sweatpants. I decided to size up to an XL in case they shrink. Ordinarily, I wear a M or L depending on the brand, and for reference, I am 6’ tall and weigh 150 lbs. These sweatpants are medium weight, seem well made, and have a relaxed fit that is very comfortable. The waist is slightly large on me, but the rest of the fit is perfect, and there is a drawstring to keep them in place. I usually wear a 32-34” waist, and these are a bit bigger than that, which makes sense because I sized up to an XL. The pockets are medium to small in size, the length is good, and I expect these will get a lot of use.

They are ok. It has a bit of an itchy feel to it. Very stretchy! Really long for my height, I’m 5’5 for reference (going to need it hemmed). My photos tell you what they look like for a large. Great fit for petite curvy women to start, I’m 5’5”, 120lbs, 34-25-38. These jeans fit like a glove honestly. I saw a lot of reviews talking about the material seeming “cheap” and how these AA jeans aren’t like the ones they’ve made in the past. Maybe I was just lucky, but the jeans I got are SUPER thick and heavy. There is NO stretch, so you’ll have to break them in by just wearing them for a few hours. I’m small but pretty curvy and I love the way these jeans fit on me! They compliment my body type just right. Only issues I have are the fact that they have no stretch which makes it super uncomfortable to sit down. I would strongly advise you to size up by 1. When I originally bought a pair back in 2013, I could fit a 24 easily. This time around I decided to go with a 26 knowing my waist size would probably fit a 25. I’m so glad I followed that advice! while the jeans are a little too big in the waist(pic #3), it was nothing a belt couldn’t fix as you can see in the photos, and they flatter me very well. Had I gone smaller I wouldn’t have been able to get them over my hips, they are already snug as is. overall, I’d highly recommend these jeans. They gave me the perfect “mom jean” fit while still accentuating my curves. I’m so happy I took a chance and purchased anyway despite the reviews.

Everything about these pants! I am 5’ and I weigh 97-100 pounds it just depends! These pants around about 1 inch about my belly button and they fit like my Hollister (00) vintage mom jeans. Everyone said go up a size but I didn’t do that and I stayed my usual size which is a size 25. I was honestly thinking of sizing down but the reviews made me second guess everything but if I would’ve dozed down, they would’ve been too tight because these pants have little stretch. My Hollister jeans don’t really fit my waste fully fitted, but I don’t mind any of that because I do like to wear belts. I think if your pant size is your true pant size, for instance you’re a size 26 but you can only fit into a size 26 and not a Size 25, then maybe you should size up because the pants are were thick and aren’t very stretch at all. Also, these pants are VERY long but that was no surprise for me. It took me 6 rolls just to have it fit the leg of my length but you can always trim those down but of course the taller you are, the less amount of rolls you’ll have to do. But other than that, I do like the fit! I’m happy with the sizing I got and won’t be sizing down or up. I really hope this helps you!

Size Up - No stretch Used to own the old pair of these in 28 and read reviews to size up so I sized up to a 29 (my waist is 27.5”-28”). The 29 is slightly too big on the waist but a size smaller would have been so tight around the hips and legs. I doubt I would have been able to sit down. If you owned these jeans in the past do not expect the same pair because the fit in the hips and legs is tighter as I mentioned. These jeans have zero stretch but I knew that coming into this. Overall I love these because they are a true high rise unlike other brands’ claims of “high rise” so I will keep them. Don’t worry about the weird “V” shape in my pictures. This happens with all of my jeans because I have very high set hips and I doubt you will have this problem! I am 5’7” and 128lbs with a short torso.

These are THICC jeans, but definitely high-waisted Relevant measurements: 5'2", about 125-130 lb, 26" to 28" waist, 34" hips I usually don't buy jeans for myself, since I find I have a hard time finding jeans that fit me in both the waist and hips -- there's an 8-10 inch difference between them! Nevertheless, I decided that I wanted to splurge and try on some name-brand jeans for once in my life. I ordered the size 27W/32L in Medium Wash. I don't like these jeans. I'll start off with the positives. They are well-constructed, and I doubt they'll fall apart anytime soon. They are definitely the most high-waisted jeans I've ever purchased for myself, which is what I was looking for. And, surprisingly enough, there wasn't a ton of excess fabric bunching up by my ankles -- a problem I commonly have as a 5'2" woman. The color of the jeans is great, and they have an almost "rustic" feel to them, in my opinion. The price is what you'd expect for something by AA. But the negatives, oh boy. The most prominent one: these jeans are THICK and NOT STRETCHY at all. It was a pain trying to get my feet and ankles through the cuffs -- and an even bigger pain trying to get them out. These aren't jeans you can just "slip on" in the morning -- it'll take you a minute to get them on. Then, of course, the thickness -- it means quality, but it also means that I feel constricted when walking and sitting down, to the point where it's actually uncomfortable. Then, even though my waist is a 26", there were still 2-3 inches of gapping between my waist and the jeans. Conclusion: I'm returning these jeans.

Very long, would be great on very tall pear shapes I'm typically an 8 or a 10 because my thighs are somewhat curvy, and read all the reviews and decided on a 30 and two 31s. I'm so sick of stretch in jeans and cropped lengths so was hopeful. Unlike others, I didn't find these to run that crazy small. They are made for a semi-curvy pear shape. My waist is about 30" and my thighs 41". Black 31's were not bad in the waist but big in the thighs, the dark wash 31s were too big, the dark wash 30's I'm keeping. If you have a similar proportion and are sick of waist gaps these are worth considering if the price is right.The denim material itself seems fine but the stitching is not that well done and the thread quality looks poor. I can update when I wear them but I'd thrift actual 90s jeans vs paying over $40 for these. They are not quality wise as good as ones from the 90s but where these differ is they are slimmer in the leg than most 90s jeans and are higher waisted than a number of them as well. I'm tired of companies today calling an 11" rise high. Not for a long torso. These are 13" on the 30's and 31's and actually hit the smallest part of my waist which is the whole point of a high rise!The bummer is they are so long. I wish they looked on me like some of the other reviewers. I'm 5'8" so I'm not short but I did have to roll them twice like the stock photo and it was bulky. Perhaps smaller sizes are shorter or they shrink in the wash? All the ones I bought had an inseam around 33.5". The amount of bulk is going to look bad if you're under 5'6". I might consider hemming them as my preference is not cuffing at all to keep them looking sleek.The black ones in the photo are size 31 in case it helps you on sizing. I feel like they measure exactly what their stated size is when pulled to their max - I.E. the 31's are 15.5" flat and closer to 15" relaxed.

Very soft, and super stretchy denim! NOTE: I don't typically wear shirts tucked in, but for this review I felt I needed to show the fit better. Don't judge too harshly! :) Size Reference: Ordered XXL. I am 5'5", 180 pounds, a 35" waist and 46.5" hips. Weight is carried in my hips and rather large backside. I highly recommend checking the size picture before ordering. I ordered the XXL according to my largest measurement, my hip size. I always find I have a waist gap anyway, so I expected a bit of gap here. Lately I have been struggling more to find pants that fit my hips without highlighting every single fat globule under my skin. If I had realized how stretchy the denim was, I would have ordered an XL. There is so much stretch at the front right at the belly area that it just gathers there. When I sit down I have a big denim poof sticking up. The back has the expected gap, but the thighs fit nicely. There are no belt loops, so there is no cinching the waist in like that. I will likely sew in some darts at the back to help with that. The stretch is so stretchy that I don't even have to unbutton these to take my pants off. I just pulled them right off, no problems. Hopefully giving my measurements and showing pictures of my body proportion will help you as you look to order the right size. I love how soft and stretchy they are, but I definitely needed a smaller size!

S/M and L are the same size; extra fabric/gathering above the ankle I ordered the S/M and the L since I'm between a M/L in leggings and don't like a waistband that's too binding. Oddly, when I opened the packaging, they looked exactly the same! I placed one on top of the other, and the rise, inseam, waist, hip, and ankle are identical (see photos of them stacked one on top of the other). I'm pretty sure these are one-size leggings that they've slapped two sizing tags onto because "one-size leggings" sound too good to be true. That said, the L fits me (31" waist, 40" hip) and I had my XXS daughter try on the S/M and they fit her 22" waist without gapping, so there's some magical stretchiness happening here. Length-wise, we're both 5'2" so there's a bit of extra length at the ankle but we can hike them up so it doesn't pool. Pros: - Sturdy exterior that looks like it will hold up to washing, soft fleecy interior (this must be the "velvet" they're referring to as they're NOT velvet on the outside, a bit like a teddy bear on the inside though), very comfy - Not so thick that it makes my legs look bulky, though there's a bit of puffy folding behind my knees and under the bum - Wore outside for an hour at 36F and I can feel the breeze through them but stayed warm - The waist band stays up; no muffin top Cons: - The front and back are identical, so there's not much room for a booty—i.e. the back side takes some coaxing to come up to mid-rise on me, though the front is easily high rise. No issue riding down throughout the day though. - They're oddly jogger-like at the ankle. More pronounced on my daughter since she has skinnier ankles. I tucked the extra fabric into my boot socks, but it's a little odd-looking with exposed ankles. Great for the $13 sale price and we're keeping both pairs.

Exactly what I was looking for OK here’s the real deal on this shirt, after reading multiple reviews. I am 5‘7“ and 125 pounds. Someone else my weight/height bought an XS and said it was perfect, and you can see in her pic, her sleeves are too short. I am a true small most of the time (or med.) and this shirt is the right fit in my size. I wanted something more fitted, this shirt has princess seams on the back. Yes it smells a little funny, so wash it! The mid blue is the perfect color for a slightly faded vintage look. The fabric isn’t true denim which it shouldn’t be as a shirt, it’s more like a lighter chambray, but it has the look of denim, which is great for me, I didn’t want it stiff. You can wear it open as a shacket, but it won’t provide much warmth. I wanted to wear it as a shirt. It’s 100% cotton so I expect a little shrinkage, but to me, it would fit even better. [Update: washed/dried, it shrank a bit and fits better! Mostly sleeve and shirttail length.] If you want it more oversized, I would suggest going up as size. I love fashion and can’t wait to style this in endless ways! It’s SO the classic that belongs in every woman’s closet, that’s why they call it wrangler retro!. The pocket placement and size is perfect for us bustier girls.

Not knee length for 5'5" frame. Better suited to taller individuals I was so excited when I received the Belle Poque Denim Skirt. It looked beautiful on the model in the photos. Unfortunately for me, that was not the case. I'm 5'5", and this skirt fell to just above my ankles (see photo). Granted, the model looks like she's wearing this above her naval - I found that if I hiked the skirt up just as high, that I was looking way off proportion. In addition, the sizing is off. I wear a size 12 and have a waist size of 34. I ordered per the sizing chart, which recommended an XL. Unfortunately, the XL skirt fits me pretty loosely - and on top of that, this skirt has a decent amount of stretch in it. I wanted to love this skirt, but to make it fit well - I'd have to take it to a tailor to have it shortened to my knee length. I deducted 1 star for the inaccurate sizing chart and somewhat misleading "knee length" in the title. Despite these issues, the fabric feels nice and soft. One note: after it's washed and dried - you may need to iron over the bottom hem as it likes to flip up.

Pleasant Surprise for Midsize person I am 5’8”, approx 170lbs with a 30” waist and 40” hips. I struggle to find pants - jeans specifically - that fit me how I like, and usually don’t have a ton of luck online. I took a chance on these and I’m so glad. Are they perfect? No - I wish the zipper was about 2” longer because when I first went to put them on I thought there was no way they were fitting, but once they were up they fit my waist and have the perfect amount of room through my hips and thighs that I have been looking for. They check a lot of boxes for me, and are even ever-so-slightly long which I never find anymore, and is one of my favorite things to have in loose jeans. I ordered my usual size, 12, again being skeptical, but it’s just right. They fit to my waist without digging in - I had abdominal surgery a year ago and now have a belly pooch that makes most jeans uncomfortable - but I can wear these all day. Also I haven’t had to touch it, but I love that they have the adjustable strap at the waist in back to get just the right fit.

Not for short gals but great pockets I am 5’ 3” and weight 135lbs with an hourglass shape. I ordered an 8 since my waist is about 28 inch and my hips are 39 inch. If you’re short like me, either wear some tall heels or hem the pants. I expect to take off a good 4 inches off of mine. The material is cotton and upon first wear is stiff. I expect them to soften with use. The pants are nicely dyed with good contrast for what is a cow print and come with deep pockets! I can fit my entire hand down the front pockets and the silhouette remains flattering. I think the sizing is true to size just not accounting for petites. The pants take a nice fit to my bottom so that’s a win in my book. The rise of the pants is around the waist which many women like for the tummy control. Overall, would recommend for ladies 5 ft 6in and taller.

Nice jeans, will be even better with some adjustments I’m 5’5,” 165 lbs, my waist measures 36” & my hips are 44.” Per the size guide provided, a 14 would be ideal, but the largest size available is 12. I hoped with a fiber content of 75% cotton & 25% polyester there’d be some stretch - so I ordered the sz 12. Well, these fit, barely (waist-wise). There is zero stretch to these jeans. Although I love the look of these, the waist is definitely snug. Fortunately I sew well, so I have a few tricks up my sleeve. (Or I could lose those last pesky 10lbs). I received these through Amazon Vine & returns/exchanges or not an option. However, even If those options were available to me, I’m not sure I’d return these - the hips & everywhere else (like my bum) fit super nice. I really like the thick cuffs which are super on trend right now, although I’ll most likely sew them securely all around. The cuffs are currently only stitched in place in a few spots (pls see image). These do run long - I’m actually wearing 2.5” wedges in my image. Those deep cuffs could come in handy for anyone who wanted these shortened (or lengthened). I love the style of the front pockets & especially the placement of the back ones. The overall quality of these are excellent. I feel the price of these are fair, especially when compared to one of my fav brands for jeans, Free People. There’s a pair of jeans very similar to these by FP I’ve had my eye on, but they’re $128. I believe with a few tweaks I’ll be very happy with these. I do recommend these jeans but highly suggest following the size guide provided.

Fit great but won’t last long for thick thighs I was surprised that it fit nicely everywhere. Usually jeans that can go over my thighs are loose on my waist. It looked just like product photo but the jeans are thin to be stretchy. If you thick thighs like me, I know this will chafe and create holes in thigh area down the road. I wanted to try it for a 70s party and loved the big bell bottom pant legs. I am 5’7” and 150 lbs and ordered the M.

SURPRISED These jeans took me by surprise. I got the M–4/6 size based off my measurements. From the comments I was expecting no belt loops and super cheap fabric. What I actually got was a nice pair of jeans that were a little tight on my thighs but not terribly uncomfortable, belt loops, and some decent fabric. I am 5’1” so they are pretty long and in the pictures I’m wearing 4 inch heels. I was also expecting a big gap in the back but there really wasn’t one and if there is it’s pretty small. Overall, they’re a good pair of jeans but not the best I’ve ever had.

I am about 5'9 and these feel a little bit short for me, would have loved an extra inch of length. UPDATE: Sturdy AF!! LOVE these leggings! I ordered the XXL and was sent 4XL. But I'm glad, because after trying them on, I don't think the XXL would have fit. The 4XL clings but isn't tight which is what I wanted. Upon going back to the product images, I'm kind of wondering how those size models fit into these? I'm 5'3'' 195lbs and I have a pear shape. Measurements hips: 48.9 inches. Waist: 40.9 inches. While taking a picture for review, I noticed a rip on a seam near the hip.. It seems the fabric is doubled so it's not actually a hole. I think I'll just fabric glue it shut and call it a day. UPDATE: I've had these leggings for several months now and they're my go to because of the pocket, high waist and comfortable fit. I just wore them to my trip to NYC and it was wild. While trying to navigate to my seat on the bus as it's moving and being tossed around; the stuff in my pocket got seriously caught on the seat handles not once, not twice, but THREE times and it yanked SO hard I swore my pants were done for! To my surprise I look down and they look absolutely untouched! Still completely in tact! That day I swore I had to update my review and express how sturdy and good quality these are! For that price, what a steal. I'm so happy with these! Get them!! I just ordered 2 more pairs. :D

Perfect balance of high waisted but not mom jeans! I'm 5'8, roughly 160lbs, very pear shaped with long legs, ordered size 8. I took a huge risk buying something not rated yet, but I'm so glad I did! These are immediately becoming a staple in my wardrobe!

Love, overall! This are actually sis jeans! I was worried they would be that cheap thin material you often see with stretch jeans but these have a nice thickness! The pockets are amazingly deep. To put it in perspective my fingers are long enough that I can palm a basket ball but if I push my hands down to the bottom of the pockets, the pocket comes up to above my wrist bone. The zipper is nice and sturdy, as is the button. My only thing I wish is that they were about an inch or so longer. I'm almost 5'11 and I think they look a little short on me. Also the crotch on these is long to me, almost like these are means jeans, bit maybe that's just how cargo pants typically fit. Overall, these are very well made and look good. FYI, they look tiny out the package but have a lot of stretch so don't let the put of package look scare you!

Color is insainely DIFFERENT! Sizing off as well I am making this short and (un)sweet..: DONT WASTE YOUR MONEY OR TIME Below are my reasons • THE COLOR DOES NOT EVEN REMOTELY RESEMBLE THE PROVIDED PHOTO OF “MAUVE” as you can tell in my picture I provided these look like totally different leggings, I purchased MAUVE what I thought was a beige/pink blush tinted or light pink leggings, what I received are dark brown(ish) with a purple tint.. it’s not even in the same color category, I’m ok with getting something that is “similar” to the color but this is on a whole other level, and yes.. I even double checked to make sure (1) I ordered the correct color (2) was shipped the item I purchased. I checked the tags, the label on the package and also amazon listing itself ... NOTE TO SELLER: to possibly enhance your reviews maybe update your listing provided pictures to be more accurate on actual color(s) in efforts to please your customer and prevent so many returns. (You can use my picture I provided if you want it’s pretty accurate) • SIZING IS OFF it’s hard to explain what I mean I realize these are One Size.. which means they should have a substantial amount of stretch to them, these do not AT ALL I have tons and tons of OS leggings and all fit fine not matter how big or small I am these are laughable (I am 5ft 174 lbs) they won’t go up the thigh and I have smaller thighs then norm. • THICK FLEECE (not good to wear for spring or summer months) I wouldn’t consider these versatile Type of leggings, they are just way to thick for comfort. SUMMARY: thanks to amazon there is a plethora of leggings options and colors and styles you can choose from, tons of sellers and all are one size (accurately) with rainbow full of colors all hovering the same price, I would stick to another brand in my opinion, just because a listing has over a 1,000 review it doesn’t necessarily mean they are worthy... these may be the worst leggings I have ever purchased unfortunately (and I have 79 pairs of leggings to date)

Non See-Thru Toffee colored leggings & really well made My favorite thing is how well made they seem to be. They look like your everyday leggings with that material ur nails/dry skin snags easily on. However when I flipped the waist down & checked the inside fabric, it was completely fleece lined as promised. I can’t believe what a great deal these are. $10, compared to another company my daughter sent me around the holidays for a gift idea, they were charging $29 + shipping for one pair of fleece lined leggings & only offer in 4 colors. Also super amazing delivery time 2-3days!I’m 5’3 150lb & they fit so amazingly perfect. I typically wear a 12 in Jrs which is the last size b4 being considered a plus size so I got the plus size just to be safe. I am not usually comfortable wearing anything skin tight. It’s not very flattering in my opinion, plus I tend to get overheated easily so the thought of something stuck to me when I’m a sweaty mess is not my idea of comfortable. So I can understand everyone’s obsession with everything leggings/jeggings, they’re so damn easy & u never will have to un-do ur top pant buttons ever again after u eat or feel bloated, they suck u in everywhere! They can be casual, workout gear, dressy with a tightly fitted sweater or blouse, even leggings Under a dress! “What’s not to like?” is my new motto. I don’t think they’re too hot to wear being fleece lined but it’s only 64 in PA. so that’s hard to tell just yet. I am a changed woman & still hold some reserve when & where u wear these,don’t think they should be acceptable funeral attire or for a job interview but with that being said-I plan on buying these in every available color I find , and my favorite part is all the millions of prints & patterns they Have out now. I would absolutely recommend, size is true to description as well as color! I did NOT receive any promotional codes, free clothes/product, browser traffic.

Overall, I like them. I'll start of with the fact that I'm 5'4" with a 29" inseam and about 185 lbs. I carry my weight in my belly and thighs. I purchased these to wear while walking my dogs in the winter. I bought the L\XL size. They're a bit long on me, but that's fine. The fit is great. Not too loose, not too tight. No muffin top. In the photos, I pulled one leg down to the middle of my foot so you get an idea of the length. They are high waisted, roughly 3" above my belly button (I'm touching my belly button in the one photo). The waist is a wide one and I haven't had any problems with it rolling down at all. The material is a little shiny but more knit than one of those slick athletic fabrics. It's a thin fleece too. I think I was expecting more of an athletic legging with thicker fleece. I just walked the dogs. It's 42F outside with a 10mph wind. My legs stayed warm. Hoping these hold up in colder temps. If not, I'll just throw on some silky long johns underneath too. Product was received on time as ordered.

Soft and warm I ordered these because they were the only burnt orange color I could find to match my fall sweatshirt, but it matches perfectly! They are warm and very soft and you can't see through them. The only downsides are the price, they are a little long (I'm 5'3") and looser around the ankles then I like and the piling of fabric after only one wear.

Seem to be good leggings but might be one size fits all? I ordered a Small/Medium which fit well but the tag says one size so not sure if that means one size fits all? Anyway, great maternity jeans, just consider sizing down because they will likely stretch out upon wearing and be roomier. *photos from first try-on

Seems to run large. My daughter is 5'1" and around 95lbs. Granted she is small, she generally can wear XS/S women's clothing. Very large and boxy Really can’t decide if I’m going to keep it - I was looking for something a little more modern / trendy, but maybe I’m just middle aged now, haha, because it looks like something my high school students would wear. It feels like an odd fit - really boxy when I’m used to more fitted, and really wide in the body. The sleeves are so long - like to the middle of my thumb. I’m 5’7”, 220 lb, size 16 (bust 44, waist 36) and I ordered XL. I could have sized down which surprised me. Maybe I’d actually like that fit better. My friend LOVES it and says keep, and my husband says it looks like an “art project”. I like that the pearls and rhinestones are attached really well, like shot through the fabric like a permanent earring if that makes sense, not glued or sewn, so they seem like they’ll last. Another reviewer said to wear your hair up or it’ll get stuck - true! Maybe I should keep it to update my boring wardrobe. I think it’ll look great with black denim and a T-shirt, or a simple T-shirt dress. I love that it’s soft denim and not too thick and stiff. Okay, guess I just talked myself into keeping it!

I LOVE THEM, but the flaws are hard to ignore On first try-on I LOVE these jeans! So much! They’re the first jeans that really fit me since pregnancy. But shortly after wearing them, the flaws really started to show: 1- the belly cover is nude/skin color (why??) it makes it so you absolutely have to have a long shirt to cover it, or it looks really weird 2- the jean area barely comes up enough to cover your crotch area. I don’t know why they didn’t bring the jeans line up higher on my stomach, it would have made it easier to wear with normal shirts. And when I sit down, it says low on my bottom too. Not super flattering. 3- they don’t stay up very well. With the stretchy fabric, and the jeans not coming up very high, they just sag a bit. This is definitely not a deal breaker, just a side note. Overall these jeans fit SO good on my legs, and made me feel happy to wear jeans again. I’m currently 140 lbs (120 pre pregnancy) and I’m 6 months pregnant. If you have lots of long shirts, it’s definitely worth it to have nice jeans! I probably would have gone with the dark wash next time, as the stretchy material matches the jean color better. I’m sad that the medium color has a nude stretchy belly, but maybe they can change that in the future.

Small fits me well as a 5'4 / 130 pound preggo (with a big belly) I've been wearing these pants for a couple of weeks now, and while they are a bit firmer than my other Motherhood Maternity skinny pants, they are still pretty comfortable. I wouldn't call these stretchy. For reference, I'm 5'4 and currently weigh probably around 130 pounds. I'm 5 months pregnant but I'm showing as though I were already 7 or 8! Pictures included for reference on fit. I was a little confused when I got these because they are in fact labeled as "Secret Fit Belly" but the actual pants label on the back says 'Branch & Twig' instead of Motherhood Maternity. Not sure if I got the right ones? But based on the photos, they look the same. These fit me well, not too short or not too long. I love these pants I love the feel of the material and they are very stretchy. I am 5'5" and 190 lbs. I first ordered a size 3X and they were too big. I returned them and got a size 2X and they fit better, but I think I could have bought a 1X and they probably would fit better. I feel so comfortable in them and the waist is not tight at all. In that department, they are great. The only thing is the length of the pants. The length is too long as you can see in the pictures. Even when I have them pulled way up, they still go to the floor. Usually, when I buy pants, the length is always just right. To get these off the floor, I have to pull them way up, BUT ... The waistband is so tall that I can first pull them way up and then just gently push the waist to where it should be, and the crotch stays where it belongs too. I really do like the comfort of the pants and I can go anyplace in these not feeling over or underdressed.

What I was looking for! UPDATE: 6/7 - I just received my order for the second pair I bought. Since I loved the first ones ( see original review below) I decided to buy more. I have another pair on the way and I hope they fit like the first. These that I got today are black with blue specks all over them and peachy pink and white flowers all over. Not the cutest but as comfortable as I thought they’d be, I don’t really care too much. Well…..these things were tight! Like I’ll have ZERO room as my belly (and probably hips too) begin to grow more. I’m disappointed. I’m not even going to keep them because they won’t last me even month I’m sure. So I’ll be doing yet another return. We’ll see how the ones that are scheduled to arrive tomorrow will fit. 6/4/22—16 weeks pregnant with twins. I don’t always feel like wearing leggings. I have big legs, big ankles and I just don’t always feel confident enough to wear leggings. Some days I want to throw some baggy pants on other than yoga or sweat pants. They’re a little long but I don’t care too much. They’re thin and the waist doesn’t dig into my stomach at all. They’re pretty loose and airy and I feel like they’ll be great in the heat. They’re not stretchy but I still have room to grow. I’m also looking for something with busy prints so I can let my jiggle, jiggle, and not wear tights and all that. I’ll grab a few more pairs. Oh…I got a 3X and they’re still loose but I’m Ok with that. I’m about 230 lb, 5’3”.

Meh. I'm not in love with it. The fit is a little weird. Im 5'3 and fluctuate between 128-135. I got the 121 Little flower in a small. Material: The fabric isn't as stretchy and the feel is not flowy or soft. You definately feel the synthetic polyester against your skin. Since its a bit stiff you can't really move or do a lunge comfortably without feeling strain. The Cut: The top half is about 11.5 in. from crotch to waistband. I like high waist bottoms but if you have a short torso I wouldnt recommend it. Sure you can fold it over but the top garter which is very tapered has no give and it will bite into any soft spots in your tummy area. Any bulges the fabric will stretch and thin showing a lighter slightly see through spot. The pant leg is very long. 29" inseam and looks more like a straight pant leg than a bell bottom flow. Sure you can hem it, but you will lose any slight bell bottom shape. Design: The print is beautiful which is why I gave it more stars than I would have otherwise. The color is bright and crisp an no bleed throughs. My only gripe is that the print is not flattering to my butt. Im not sure if this makes sense but the print has a more of a camouflage effect to make things bumps appear smaller. This might also have something to do with the cut falling straight down from the hip instead of your thigh like a bell bottom should. I think even if I size up the cut will still be wierd and any hemming I can do will just make the pants boxier. I want to give it a shot because of the pretty print but its just not what I was looking for so I will be return it.

Kinda cheap and not necessarily stretchy These were cute, but not quite as expected compared to other leggings/stretchy pants I’ve ordered. The material was pretty thin and cheap feeling/looking. I am 5’8”, 140lbs and ordered a M, which is usually on point. These were a little too big and the top of the waistband felt like it didn’t give. The length was actually really nice because I can never find pants long enough, but the waist and legs were a bit too baggy. I probably could size down to a S, but am not because of the material and waistband (a small might be too small at the top and not give). I also wasn’t expecting the inside to be white material on a mostly black pair. Returned without getting a new one.

I need these pants in every color and print!!! I literally have NOTHING bad to say about these pants. The material is soooo buttery silky! The top of the pants rises to just above my belly button. I can even pull them higher to make the leg length shorter so I can wear flats with them. Ok, so for reference: I’m 36 years old, bra size is about a 40DDD or E (not really sure; I don’t even wear bras anymore), 5’6” and weigh about 290lbs. I ordered the XXXL. It fits beautifully. It’s not snug in the waist so I probably could’ve gone down a size if I wanted a tighter fit. I wear camis with built in bras that also help to tighten and flatten my tummy so that’s probably another reason why these fit so good in my waist. I’m very curvy and have some nice size hips and these pants just flatter all of it! I love the loose legs. You can dress them up or down (apparently they’re called pajama pants???). I apologize for the picture. My full length mirror is in my daughter’s playroom. I got the purple pants and paired it with one of my yellow tops. This is now my go-to outfit for all LSU games! GEAUX TIGERS 🐯 I just can’t express how much I love these pants. I’m ordering another pair as soon as I’m done writing this review!!

Lightweight and flowy - great for summer Size small 5’5”, 146-150, 32DDD, These feel like loose fit pajama pants, so if you want a heavyweight fabric skip them. I like the lightweight feel, especially for summer. They will definitely show panty lines so thong or commando required. They’re ankle length on me so I’ll wear flats, but the waist is very elasticized and would be easy to wear a couple inches lower if you want to pair with heels. They’re high waisted (sit about an inch above my bellybutton) so good for crop tops too. But if you’re looking for tummy control or thick fabric they won’t be for you.

Skip if you’re 5’2” or less These would be great lounge pants. I’m not sure I could pass them off as work pants due to the shiny yoga pant material but that would depend on where you work. In a professional environment? These aren’t the pants you’re looking for. Additionally if you are 5’2” or below like me, the inseam will likely be too long. After reading reviews from people who were my height or less, I thought I would be able to wear these with flats to do school pickups or run quick errands. The pants were so long! Even with three inch heels, my pants caught on my feet. I do have small size 5 feet so the pant legs totally cover my foot. Since the inseam measurements in reviews and answers are all over, I think they must not be consistent. On the pair I received, the inseam was 32” and overall length was 43.5” which is just a tad longer than the description. My recommended size was a large but based on reviews, I ordered a medium and they still felt large. I’m undecided if I want to try the small, I doubt they would fit into my 42” hips comfortably. 🤣

Shockingly tall enough!!! Odd to refer to a pair of pants as life-changing I know but I am 6 ft tall and most of that is all legs & as a result I stopped wearing pants years ago because it just wasn't worth it to try to find ones that fit me right. I literally just don't wear them. Whenever I have found pants that are long enough they fit all wrong everywhere else and pants that fit in the waist and butt are always at least 4 inches too short. These are honestly the first pants that have fit correctly and that I have worn in many years. They're stretchy and comfortable and plenty long. I don't know how shorter people would with these pants as again I am 6 feet tall and they fit me perfectly but I couldn't be happier. The only reason I bought them is because I received a different pair of the same brand as a gift and could not believe how perfectly they fit and since then I have purchased them in five different patterns and will continue to buy more!! If you have extremely long legs and have a hard time finding pants that fit, but these now!! Game changer for us tallies!

Comfort style and quality ♥️ Absolutely love! I broke my femur. I’m tired of pj pants! Stretchy and comfortable These faux leather shorts are cute and fit well. They are pretty stretchy all over (including the waist), so they'll easily accommodate different shaped bodies. They seem well made (seams seem strong) and the zipper runs smoothly. The outside 'leather' is a little shiny (iridescent (?)) looking with a bit of a woven fabric pattern (it is not a smooth looking finish). I took an up close pic of the pocket area so you can see the material's finish. I like the way the material looks. The inside of the shorts is the backside of the 'leather' and it's a soft polyester/nylon feeling material, so the 'leather' isn't touching your skin, but the shorts are not lined with a separate material. I took a pic that I hope shows what I'm talking about.I ordered a size L (my normal size) based on their size chart and they fit comfortably (I have a 32" waist and 40" hips). They fit true to size. If you are in between sizes and want a comfortable fit, I would say size up and if you want a more skin tight fit, then size down. The tag says you can hand wash them or wash them inside out on the delicate cycle. They came pretty wrinkled (I haven't washed them yet and the pics show the way they arrived), so I think I'll need to wash and gently steam these to smooth them out. These are fun shorts that will go with a variety of tops and shoes (or knee high boots).luvamia Womens Shorts Trendy High Waisted Faux Leather Look Stretchy Jean Shorts Short Denim Pants with Pockets Comfy (Black, L)

Cute, trendy, flattering fit So far I am loving these shorts. They are lightweight, not bulky material, but not so thin that they feel cheap or show underwear lines. I am 5'2" 128 lbs, waist 29" and hips 36". I got size medium and could probably have gone with small for a more fitted pair, but based on the size chart I was a combination of small and medium measurements for waist and hips and didn't want to take the chance on it being too small/unable to zip up. Medium does not look too loose or baggy on me, but the fit is definitely looser than how many ppl would wear this style and material. Based on how they fit me and the stretchy give to the material, medium would definitely still fit someone a few pounds/several inches bigger. I do like the stretchiness to the material, but it makes me wonder if they would stretch too much after sitting in them/wearing them for an extended period of time. I haven't worn them yet except to try on, so will update this review should any of those issues occur. But so far, I'm a fan and will probably buy in more colors!

My favorite belt I work in a saw mill and have lost over 60 pounds in the last 2 years and it's been so uncomfortable trying to find belts I don't have to fight with or belts I don't break within the first months time. With me being a female suspenders just don't work for me. I started using Bungie cords to try and keep my pants up while I lift logs all day long and after more weight lose they werent working for me either. My boss told me about adjustable belts and options outside normal belts you can buy at Walmart. So...I found these belts here on Amazon and ordered them. I wore one all week last week and love it. Depending on which pants I wear and how high up and low they fit on my waist will depend on if I have to wear the metal piece that comes along with the belts. Most times with me wearing my pants below my belly button I can hook these belts to the front belt loops and won't even have to use the piece of metal to hook the belt at the zipper line of my jeans. I love not having to fight a belt or constantly pulling up my pants or trying to constantly make adjustments just to work and keep my pants where they need to be. I would definitely buy these belts again. I'm a 29 inch waist and I tightened the belt as tight as possible and it keeps my pants up and keeps it comfortable. I don't have to worry about my pants dropping. 🤣 I would definitely buy these belts again.

Very nice pants for Fall/Winter These are such nice, corduroy pants! I got them for my granddaughter, age 15, who really liked the look of them on Amazon. She’s wearing an adult size 8-10, and is very picky about what she likes to wear for school. Meaning, they had to be black. Haha. Seriously, though, black is her signature color, as she proudly proclaims. She loves that these are cargo pants, and also that they’re corduroy, rather than plain old denim. The corduroy is thin-wale, and the fabric is very soft, making it a perfect, cozy feel for Fall/Winter. The pants fit her perfectly, too. The fabric has a nice bit of stretch in the waist, but not obviously so - it’s just enough to stretch over the hips, then fit her perfectly in the waist. The length is even great! So many pants are too long on even average-size women/girls. These are a tiny bit long on my 5’4” granddaughter, but she prefers them to be long and slouchy. She’s a tough sell these days, but she is honestly delighted with these pants. The price of $34.99, as of this writing, is very reasonable for a quality-made pair of pants. These are machine wash and dry, perfect for a busy family with 3 kids. I’m very pleased with these pants.

I would have sent them back & went with a med, but I am afraid that would be too short then, so I'm mulling over just sending them back?! Haven't decided yet though!? They seem well enough made, & at prices as they are, they seems reasonable I suppose?! I like the high waist & button fly! Hubby liked them, so heck, might keep them!? LOL They ARE comfy! They'd be great fishing jeans for sure as they give when you bend & move!! They do have a strange odor though right out of the package, that I HOPE will go away after washing! Kind of a petroleum type smell?!? NOT pleasant at all though, but maybe due to the hot weather & being in plastic? But, I'm sure washing will help! All & all they are nice & would recommend. I'd say the sizing is pretty much spot on, so pay attention to that & you should be fine! Hope this helps! :-) Thanks much!

Great, considering the style This is a great relaxed “boyfriend” style overall. For all the reviews saying it’s saggy in the crotch or makes your butt look bad, I’m sorry, but that’s just the nature of overalls... especially a looser fit. And especially for those of us who grew up in the late 80s early 90s who are conditioned to think tight pants are the only pants worth wearing in public. I’m generally between a small and medium in both pants and tops. 5’6” with long legs but a short torso. I weigh 145, but am an athletic build so built about the way someone who weighs 130-135 who doesn’t lift heavy weights. I ordered a medium. The best thing you can do before returning these is take some selfies standing how you normally would for a picture and send to some of your GIRL friends. We’re usually our own worse critics, and if you’re not used to overalls or this particular fit, a second opinion helps. Don’t ask a man, they’d be happiest to see you with no pants ever. Anything that leaves your shape to the imagination to any degree annoys them 😂 They are soft and comfortable, a thinner stretchy denim. The light color is great for summer. I only gave 4 stars because out of the wash, I don’t like the way the top seam of the chest pocket sort of puckers and folds over. Easily fixable by wetting it down and ironing it(I use my straightener 😂), but annoying. Regardless, I will be buying these in the distressed style as well.

For all the reviews saying it’s saggy in the crotch or makes your butt look bad, I’m sorry, but that’s just the nature of overalls... especially a looser fit. And especially for those of us who grew up in the late 80s early 90s who are conditioned to think tight pants are the only pants worth wearing in public. I’m generally between a small and medium in both pants and tops. 5’6” with long legs but a short torso. I weigh 145, but am an athletic build so built about the way someone who weighs 130-135 who doesn’t lift heavy weights. I ordered a medium. The best thing you can do before returning these is take some selfies standing how you normally would for a picture and send to some of your GIRL friends. We’re usually our own worse critics, and if you’re not used to overalls or this particular fit, a second opinion helps. Don’t ask a man, they’d be happiest to see you with no pants ever. Anything that leaves your shape to the imagination to any degree annoys them 😂 They are soft and comfortable, a thinner stretchy denim. The light color is great for summer. I only gave 4 stars because out of the wash, I don’t like the way the top seam of the chest pocket sort of puckers and folds over. Easily fixable by wetting it down and ironing it(I use my straightener 😂), but annoying. Regardless, I will be buying these in the distressed style as well.

Measurement and comparison of "small" and "medium" sizes My waist measurement (smallest point where I might wear a belt) is 31", which is at the top of the small size and low in the medium size, so I ordered both to compare. The small size is about 38" total length (not counting the buckle), and the medium is 43" inches long. The small belt definitely fits my small waist measure; I would be using a hole in the middle of the range. However, it would be way too small to fit lower on my hips, where I sometimes wear a belt (my hip measure increases to 41" ). The medium size belt is a touch too large for my 31" waist (shown in the photo) -- I will have to punch another hole. However, I will keep this one so that I can wear it lower on my hips as well. So my advice for sizing is to take into account where you wear your belt, and if you're near the ends of the measurement ranges, size up for versatility! Too big is better than too small since you can punch another hole, or I guess shorten the whole belt by removing the buckle.

Really liking them so far These jeans are nice and stretchy. In Levi's Mid Rise Skinny jeans, I have been wearing a 10, but they are cutting me in half at the waist lately. I am about 5'4", 143 lbs. I carry my weight in my stomach, so that's where most of my "curvy" is. I ordered a 10 in these jeans, hoping the extra stretch would help in the waist. They were, indeed, very comfortable in the waist, which measured 16" flat across. But the thighs and front were kind of baggy. I sent them back and ordered two pairs of 8s and they fit much better. The waist is a bit smaller, measuring 15" across, but there is so much stretch available that it's not a problem. The butt and legs fit closer, although they are not what I would consider tight, as you can see by my picture. Inseam on the size 10 and the 8 is 30". And they are definitely high waisted on me, not mid. Maybe I'm short waisted, but they come up to just above my belly button. Kind of strange feeling at first, but even though I do not tuck my shirts in, my muffin top always wanted to show through my tops. That isn't happening as much now, so I'm liking it. The size 10 pair were the Bristol-Whiskers, a kind of light blue. The 8s I ordered are Salida and a really dark pair, Parker Whisker. Out of the 3, the Salida are my favorite. A medium blue (lighter than they appear in the picture, but darker than the 10s). The other 8s, the Parker Whisker, are extremely dark blue and I may exchange them for another Salida. The "whiskering" on all of these jeans is pretty mild, much less than it looks in the pics they show. Today is the first day of me wearing my new jeans, so I can't tell if they will bag out, but they don't seem like they will.

Perfect!! I'm 5'3" 160-165lbs, slightly muscular body composition. And I love this dress. My arms are larger in my biceps and this doesn't make my arms feel too fat etc. I really love this dress. I want to wear it everyday. So comfortable.

I’m 5‘6“ tall and my measurements are 37 inch bust 31 inch waist and 44 inch hip. I’m between a medium and large, but I decided to go with the large due to my hip size. It did fit true to size. Overall and very happy with it and I can’t wait to put my full ensemble together for the event!

I feel like I have short legs for my body, and with my shoes on, the length comes to about a 1/2 inch from the ground. My inseam is 30 inch. UPDATE: I ordered the medium and they were just too big. They fit better in the hips and the waist, but they were oddly baggy in the crotch. It looked like I was trying to smuggle the crown jewels in there. Also the inseam was about two to three inches longer on the medium. They were dragging on the floor. These are really super cute pants, but sadly I'm going to have to send both sizes back. I wish they had numbered sizes.

Sizing is a little weird for me because my waist is fluffier than I would like and I usually have to pick between waist size or hip size on a size chart. The length of perfect. I put a bootie (maybe 3” heel) and the length is perfect. I am very happy with these jeans. They are snug but stretchy and the fabric is very comfortable. Love the designs on the pant legs. Super happy with this purchase. I recommend sizing down especially if you are short. However, the thighs are a little tight. I looked at the size chart and actually measured my thigh before I ordered. If you have bigger or muscular thighs, consider measuring or stay with your normal size. Also pics aren’t great and I was only wearing one shoe

On closer inspection, it appears that they photoshopped out the white sneakers that the model was actually wearing and added in the black heels to make the pants look like they come down farther. According to the size chart, a small is 38.5" long (length of inseam isn't listed). My pair is exactly 40" long with a 32" inseam. The design is pretty and the pants are comfortable, but they are just WAY TOO SHORT!

Runs a little big, but I love them!!! I love these embroidered jeans!!! Height 5’2”, 120 lbs., waist is 29”, hips 35”. So I ordered a small based on the sizing chart, but I think an XS would fit a little better in the hips, as I would prefer the to be a little more snug in that area since I have a flat backside. The stretchy denim fits perfectly tight in my thighs as is characteristic of this cut and style. Being on the shorter side, these are about 1” too long on me. My only disappointment is that the embroidery was sewn through both front pockets. That’s too bad because the pockets are big and seem deeper than some women’s jeans pockets. But the increased pocket size doesn’t seem to make them bulky in the front like you might think. The crotch is a tiny bit too low but I don’t think anyone will notice, so it isn’t a problem for me.

Just a Bit Loose I recently received a pair of these straight-leg jeans and I have to say, they’re incredibly soft and comfortable. The high-waisted design is exactly like the pictures, and I’m really happy with the length. I went for the light blue color and ordered a size 2 based on the size chart. The fabric is a nice blend of cotton, polyester, viscose, and spandex, which adds to the comfort. However, the fit is a bit loose around my waist. For reference, I’m 5'3", 106 lbs, and my waist is 26 inches. Despite the slight sizing issue, I’m not too concerned since I love the unique gradient of the jeans. They also come with front and rear pockets, which is a nice touch. Overall, while the loose fit isn’t the most flattering for my frame, the jeans are still stylish and different. I’d give them a solid 4 out of 5 stars.

Stretchy high waisted tall jeans Can I get an amen for the TALL jeans!!!!! I love these! I am 6’1 and it’s soooo hard to find jeans that are long enough and are still flattering in all the right places, but these are amazing! They actually fit in both length and waist. Cute, flattering, fit close to expected. Know your measurements. I read a lot of other reviews before purchasing, mostly about fit. I have an hourglass shaped build at 5'2 and 190-200 lbs. The fabric is soft and not super stiff like denim can sometimes be. The cut of the skirt is super flattering, even if you have a belly. I could see this looking especially nice on a top heavy or straight framed body type due to how it flares from the waist. The belt is of a nice quality and the "metal" (feels like a really hard plastic) on the belt and buttons are a nice antiquated brassy color. The buttons are purely decorative and not at all functional. I got an XL based on my measurements and it was actually a little roomy, but tightening the belt fixed the issue. The belt loop has elastic in the back so it does have some stretch to it. I would definitely recommend knowing your waist/hip/leg measurements to make sure you get the right waist size, but also because this is a fairly long skirt, especially if you're short. The color is accurate to the listing (but looks lighter in my pictures). I bought this for a specific event but like it enough to keep it in my regular routine. I would say it's worth the price and would probably recommend to others.

Just as described, don't size up Use the size chart included with the photos of the coat. I'm 5'3" 124 lbs. Size chart said 44 inch chest for the small and that is correct. Cute, striped denim barrel jeans The legs of these pants are not as wide as other barrel jeans that I have, so are a bit more straight. This is a size medium and the waist is a bit tight, but good fitting everywhere else. Love the striped fabric, you don't see that a lot in denim. This could be dressed up or down with boots or heels. I'll probably hem these, as they are a bit long on me at 5'2". The front pockets are nice and deep, would fit even large phones, and the back pockets are large as well.

Adorable COMFY baggy jeans I’m 60, 5’5” and 165lbs. I carry excess weight in my tummy which is 36 and my hips are 44.” The size guide did not make much sense. It stated an XL waist measured 23.6 which to me is more like an XXS. Fortunately I’ve purchased jeans and pants from this brand, LifeShe, so I ordered my “usual” size XL in Blue. The fit is slightly loose, which is the style/fit I prefer. These do not fit me low rise, but I’m very short waisted. The waistband is approx 36” relaxed. It’s elasticized but only on the sides and I’m especially happy there are belt loops. The care tag states cotton and polyester, no percentages but it looks like all cotton and it definitely feels like all cotton. It’s lightweight and breathable but not thin or flimsy; a nice quality denim constructed very well. I can’t think of anything I dislike about these jeans - from the deep pockets (the front right has a hidden change pocket) to the contrasting stitching. I love barrel jeans/pants and have quite a few, but I’d consider these more of a reg baggy style, although they do flare out in the hips (these measure 26” laying flat side to side). The length/inseam may not be “super tall friendly.” In my image I’m wearing platform Converses that are about 1.5.” I wish these were just an inch longer in case they shrink. If they do will update my review. I received these through Amazon Vine at a discounted price in exchange for my review. However, I feel at just under $40 the price is very fair. They’re actually 10% off at the time of this review (plus free Prime shipping). I love these so much I purchased the black & white ones.

Initial Impressions I'm 5'2" (157.28cm) and 158~ lbs (71.5kg) Ordered the black pair in the size 8, tag on jean says 8/29. Purchased these to wear to work, 10hr shift in fab & assembly. First Impressions: Stretch is good, thicker fabric kinda scratchy Legs actually look longer with the bootcut, does the curves justice. Solid shade of black. No back gap, might need belt. Fit : 4/5 Fit my legs and especially my thighs and butt (the biggest part of my body) comfortably but is a little looser one my waist/stomach. I could go down a full 2 inches on the waistline but nothing a belt won't help with. The waistline sits right at my bellybutton (35" (89cm) to the floor) and doesn't ride too far down when I bend or squat, I have full coverage. I don't know if going down a size would be the right move for me just because I want them to be comfortable while I'm working and if they're too snug will hamper my range of movement. Length : 4/5 They are a little long, would go with a petite size if possible, but they will be worn with boots so it's not an issue. If you don't mind the hems dragging a bit or some hemming work like me then you'll be fine. Work pants are ment to get dirty. Fabric : 3/5 Seems like a sturdy denim. A bit scratchy but that'll be fixed with an initial wash. They do have a little stretch so if you are in between sizes then you're safe to go on the lower end. Seams are solid and the fabric is thicker than regular fashion jeans. Haven't put it through a workday yet but they seem like they'll hold up. Pockets : 2/5 Better than most jeans but could be bigger. When it comes to pockets size matters. At 16cm deep, just barely over 6", there's room for improvement where there isn't for my phone, keys, wallet, chapstick, and work fob. Can't carry a purse around the warehouse, ya feel? Price : 1.5/5 I personally don't like spending more than $20-$25 on a pair of pants but I need something sturdy and that fits over my boots. I bought these on a 20% off sale for $40 instead of $50 which is the only reason I considered these. Honestly just feels like I'm paying for the brand and not the pants at this point. I did order a pair of Wranglers that were on sale for $30 (29% off $42) that'll be here in a day or two. Depending on how well that pair fits I may return the Dickies and get another pair of W's (or 2, for that price). All that being said, I have not actually worn these to work so if I decide to keep them and they hold up for a long while then maybe they're worth it. For a $50 pair of jeans they at least better last me 3-5 years.

Lost and entire seem on day 1 of use!!! First things first the fit is NOT a perfect shape! I got the size 2 The gap in the back is going to drive me nuts!! The crotch has a weird bulge from the zipper that weirds me out, the material is incredibly itchy and doesnt feel like it will be sturdy. The last thing is the length is apparently for an Amazonian woman because its a good bit longer than my body granted I am 5'3" and I weigh 115 lbs but dickies are SUPPOSED to be a good brand. Overall not impressed. Edit: I would like to add that as im at work I just lost an entire seem in the rear end. DONT buy these they are NOT worth your money.

Basic Bootcut Jean With Imperfect Fit I like how the Dickies look on my wife and can't detect any obvious issues, but my wife feels these jeans are oddly sized. When I looked more closely with a discernible eye, I concur that it's an imperfect fit. The basic design suggests form fitting comfort and in some areas of the body, it delivers on that point. But where some areas fit right, other spots are incongruous and are not a good match on my curvy wife. First off, the size 10 R is long. My 5'5 ½" wife wore 2 inch heeled boots (see photos) and the leg hem is to the floor. In all fairness, my wife wears size 8-10 pants and perhaps the 8 would work better. These definitely run large. She needs to keep pulling them up as they slide down, even over her curvy hips. She will need to wear a belt and high heels to make them work. They look good around the legs and derriere, but are too wide on the waist and feel loose and bunchy around the hips and crotch. The actual material feels like value fabric. These are not super smooth jeans. They feel and look a wee vintage and in that regard seem like old fashioned jeans of the 80s but with a little more stretch than pure cotton pants of the past. The stitching and weave is decent and the button and zipper work great. They appear to be sturdy, breathable pants that will last a long time. The pockets are nice and deep and there is a slight stretch throughout, but almost too much stretch to the point of slippage and instability. My wife isn't sure if she will wear the Dickies much as she has other shape jeans that look and feel better. But all things considered, it's a good value for a basic classic bootcut jean for the right body type. Just bear in mind that they run long and large. ~ Kort

Perfect! I love the length!! I have long legs and need an inseam of about 31in otherwise pants would look ridiculous on me. Not only do they fit me great length wise, but they fit my shape great too! My hip and waist size only differ a little and if I want a pair of pants to fit my butt great, sometimes they end up too tight around my waist. These are looser around the waist but tight around the butt. Exactly what my body type needs. I bought a size smaller than my usual size 6. But the size 4 was too loose around the waist. So I bought a size 2 and they fit perfectly! There are also REAL pockets that are pretty deep. In one pic i have my whole hand in my pocket. I am 5'6 about 125lbs.
